RICHARD STERLING paces at the head of a sprawling mahogany table. His gaze sweeps over the seated agents like a hawk tracking field mice.

RICHARD
The ecosystem is thinning. The gazelles are whining about mortgage rates. The lions are closing in cash.

Across the table, GEMMA FROST slides a heavy, embossed slip of paper toward VALERIE VANCE. A commission check. An obscene amount of zeroes. 

GEMMA
(sweetly)
I really admire how you just keep showing up, Val. It takes so much bravery to weather a dry spell like yours.

Gemma beams. Her hands rest on the table. Her thumb aggressively digs into her index cuticle. 

She rips a jagged strip of skin. A bright bead of blood wells up. 

Valerie doesn't blink. She picks up the check by the very corner, inspecting the paper against the fluorescent light.

VALERIE
Thick stock. Woven finish. It's a shame the bank spent more on the stationery than the actual escrow margins.

GEMMA
It's a seven-figure gross. 

Gemma picks harder. A drop of blood falls, smearing against the polished mahogany. She keeps her eyes locked dead on Valerie.

GEMMA
Maybe you can finally upgrade that leased sedan. 

Valerie slides the check back across the smeared blood.

VALERIE
The depreciation on new vehicles is a tragic ROI. I prefer liquid assets. 

Richard stops pacing. He leans over Gemma's shoulder, his shadow swallowing her. He taps the stained wood.

RICHARD
You're bleeding on the veneer, Gemma. Hemoglobin ruins the finish.

Gemma blinks. Her sugary smile falters. She snatches her phone off the table, staring at her own reflection in the dark screen while blindly dabbing her bloody finger with a napkin. 

Valerie stares at the red smear left on the wood. 

SMASH CUT TO:

scenes.narrative_action: Richard Sterling turns his attention to Valerie, threatening her job with a calm, unnerving smile for losing a key demographic. Secretly drowning in massive debt from her lavish lifestyle, Valerie's professional mask finally slips. Pushed to her absolute limit of stress, she obsessively scrubs at invisible stains on her expensive cuffs, her breathing growing shallow as the room watches.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Strips away Valerie's armor, exposing her deep financial desperation and setting the stage for her fatal compromise. Micro-Conflict: (><) Richard wants to crush Valerie's confidence vs. Valerie desperately trying to hide her internal panic. Value Shift: (+/-) Valerie goes from a position of cold authority to visible, desperate vulnerability.
scenes.draft_text:
INT. CONFERENCE ROOM - CONTINUOUS

Richard Sterling sits at the head of the sprawling mahogany table. He rests his hands perfectly flat on the polished wood. 

RICHARD
In a healthy forest, Valerie, the canopy thrives by starving the underbrush of sunlight. It’s a necessary brutality. The rot feeds the roots.

Valerie Vance sits rigid. Her tailored suit is a flawless armor of success. 

VALERIE
If we factor in the Q3 market recalibration, it’s a turnkey transition. The depreciation in the tech sector is--

RICHARD
You lost the demographic. 

The room goes dead silent. 

Gemma Frost doesn't look up. She checks her reflection in her phone’s dark screen, slowly reapplying a sheer coat of lip gloss.

Toby Higgins stares at the table, compulsively nudging his three highlighters until they form a perfect ninety-degree angle.

RICHARD
(calm, smiling)
The newly-wealthy tech sector is a buffet, and you just let a twenty-million-dollar whale swim into another agency's net. 

Valerie’s chest rises and falls. A fraction too fast. Her thumb drifts down to her left wrist. 

She begins to rub the edge of her pristine white cuff. Scrubbing an invisible stain.

VALERIE
The buyer pool lacked structural foundation. I am... curating a more exclusive clientele. If you look at the craftsmanship of our current high-yield properties, the aesthetic value alone guarantees a premium ROI.

RICHARD
Curating. That’s a beautiful word. 

Richard tilts his head. His smile doesn't reach his eyes. 

RICHARD
I’m restructuring the top floor. Pruning the dead branches. 

Valerie scrubs the silk cuff harder. Her thumb digs into her own wrist bone. 

VALERIE
My pipeline is robust. The equity I’ve built in this firm--

RICHARD
Equity is an illusion until it’s cashed out. 

Richard stands. He buttons his suit jacket.

RICHARD
If you don't bring me a marquee listing by Friday, your territory goes to Gemma. Your desk will be cleared by lunch. 

Valerie’s breathing turns shallow. A faint wheeze escapes her throat. 

She stares at the cuff. The skin beneath it is flushed, raw red. 

VALERIE
(voice trembling)
The territory is... I am highly leveraged in the upcoming quarter. You can't.

RICHARD
Friday, Val. Bring me meat. Or become it.

Richard walks out. Gemma smirks, finally locking her phone. 

Valerie sits perfectly still. Stripped bare. Trapped.

CUT TO:

scenes.narrative_action: Sensing blood in the water and his own fragile ego threatened by her defiance, Richard's sophisticated facade breaks. He leans directly into Valerie's personal space, physically trapping her in her chair. He whispers his threats through clenched teeth, pushing her toward the brink of absolute professional humiliation.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: The climax of the sequence's pressure cooker, forcing Valerie into a corner where she has no choice but to make a drastic, reckless move. Micro-Conflict: (><) Richard uses physical intimidation to break Valerie vs. Valerie's desperate need to survive the corporate ladder. Value Shift: (-/-) Valerie is completely cornered, her career seemingly over.
scenes.draft_text:
INT. CONFERENCE ROOM - MOMENTS LATER

Valerie sits rigid at the sprawling mahogany table. 

Richard’s signature, unnerving smile vanishes. The sophisticated patriarch is gone. 

He rounds the table, planting both hands hard onto the armrests of Valerie’s chair. He cages her in. The leather creaks under his weight.

RICHARD
(whispering through clenched teeth)
You think you can disrupt the ecosystem, Val? In the wild, the parasite doesn't outlive the host. It just starves.

Valerie’s thumb finds the edge of her French cuff. She scrubs at a microscopic, invisible stain on the silk. Hard.

VALERIE
The modernist property has unparalleled market velocity. The architectural pedigree alone guarantees a record-breaking escrow--

RICHARD
I am not talking about the concrete.

He leans in closer. His tie brushes her shoulder.

RICHARD
I am talking about your desk being cleared out by security before lunch. I am talking about you walking out of this lobby with nothing but a cardboard box and the clothes you rented.

Valerie’s thumb rubs her cuff so frantically the skin underneath flushes red. She stares straight ahead. 

VALERIE
You need my client roster to close the quarter. It's a symbiotic asset.

RICHARD
Your clients are my clients. You are just the tour guide.

He pushes off the chair, straightening his bespoke suit. The calm, predatory smile returns.

RICHARD
Hand over the keys by noon, or I'll make sure you never sell a parking space in this city again.

He turns and exits, the heavy glass door clicking shut behind him. 

Valerie is left alone in the cavernous room. Her breathing turns shallow. She keeps scrubbing the cuff.

SMASH CUT TO:

scenes.narrative_action: Desperate to maintain her ruthless facade and survive, Valerie violently slams her hand on the mahogany table, breaking Richard's physical hold. She bluffs her way into claiming she has secured the exclusive listing for a mysterious, newly built modernist mansion on the edge of town. The room falls dead silent, her aggressive triumph temporarily holding the wolves at bay.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Concludes the Set-up beat. Valerie commits a desperate sin (the bluff) that will directly lead her to the literal monster in the next sequence. Micro-Conflict: (><) Valerie takes a massive, lying risk vs. the room's predatory skepticism. Value Shift: (-/+) Valerie regains temporary power and survives the meeting, but seals her dark fate.
scenes.draft_text:
INT. CONFERENCE ROOM - CONTINUOUS

Richard leans over Valerie, trapping her against the heavy mahogany table. His grip on her upper arm is rigid. White-knuckled.

RICHARD
(whispering)
The ecosystem is shifting, Val. If your pipeline is dry, the firm absorbs your desk. It’s not malice. It’s just gravity.

Across the table, Gemma slowly drags a tube of lip gloss across her mouth, studying her reflection in her phone screen.

GEMMA
Honestly, a fresh start somewhere smaller might be good for you, babe. Less pressure.

Under the table, Valerie’s free hand obsessively scrubs at an invisible stain on her pristine silk cuff. Her breathing hitches. 

She stares at the polished wood grain. Calculates. 

BAM.

Valerie violently slams her palm onto the mahogany. 

The gunshot echo snaps through the room. Richard flinches, stepping back. His physical hold breaks.

VALERIE
My pipeline is fully leveraged, Richard.

Valerie stands. She smooths her skirt, her icy professional veneer snapping back into place.

VALERIE
I secured the Belvedere Ridge property. The new modernist build on the edge of town. 

Dead silence. Gemma lowers her phone.

GEMMA
That’s impossible. The developer isn't taking meetings. 

VALERIE
He took mine. 

Valerie steps toward the door, projecting pure, transactional confidence. 

VALERIE
It’s a turnkey architectural marvel. High-yield, off-market. I have the exclusive listing. 

Richard studies her face. Searching for the lie. 

RICHARD
Bring the signed exclusivity agreement to my office by five. Or you're a fossil. 

VALERIE
Have Toby draft the premium collateral. I don't show brutalism without high-res gloss. 

She grabs her briefcase. She strides through the frosted glass doors, leaving the wolves staring in silence.

SMASH CUT TO:

scenes.scene_heading: INT. REAL ESTATE AGENCY - HALLWAY - CONTINUOUS
scenes.narrative_action: Valerie bursts out of the conference room, the heavy door clicking shut behind her. The adrenaline of her triumphant bluff fades instantly. She leans against the cool wall, pulling out her phone to check her banking app: severely overdrawn. She spots Toby down the hall and immediately barks an order at him to secure the lockbox code for the rumored modernist property before Richard can verify her lie, masking her deep financial panic with an icy, transactional professionalism.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: Bridges the previous sequence's bluff into actionable momentum, forcing Valerie to make her lie a reality. Micro-Conflict (><): Valerie needs to secure access to the house immediately vs. the risk of Toby or Richard discovering her desperate bluff. Value Shift (+/-): From the triumphant high of her conference room victory (+) to the crushing anxiety of her financial reality (-).
scenes.draft_text:
INT. REAL ESTATE AGENCY - HALLWAY - CONTINUOUS

The heavy frosted-glass door of the conference room CLICKS shut.

VALERIE stands alone in the corridor. The icy, triumphant smile instantly vanishes from her face.

Her shoulders drop. She leans heavily against the cool plaster. 

She pulls out her phone. Swipes to her banking app.

A glaring red number: -$8,412.50.

Valerie stares at the negative balance. Her breathing turns shallow. 

With her free hand, she begins to furiously scrub at an invisible stain on the cuff of her immaculate silk blouse. Scrubbing. Digging into the expensive fabric.

Footsteps approach.

Valerie’s head snaps up. The phone vanishes into her blazer. Her posture snaps back to rigid, flawless symmetry.

TOBY rounds the corner. He balances a thick stack of escrow files against his chest.

VALERIE
Toby. The modernist property on the ridge. I need the master lockbox code. Right now.

Toby blinks, pausing mid-stride.

TOBY
The Blackwood estate? But Mr. Sterling hasn’t officially processed the intake--

VALERIE
Sterling operates on a legacy timeline. We are moving at market speed. The code.

TOBY
It’s not in the MLS yet. To get the code, I’d have to bypass the primary brokerage portal.

VALERIE
Then bypass it.

TOBY
But that generates an automated anomaly report to the regional director. Are you sure we shouldn't--

VALERIE
Do you understand the intrinsic value of a cantilevered foundation, Toby?

Toby shifts his weight.

TOBY
I mean, structurally, it distributes the load--

VALERIE
It depreciates the second it sits stagnant. It requires an aggressive steward to maximize the aesthetic yield. We are securing the asset before another firm realizes it exists. 

Toby looks down at his stack of folders. He taps the edges against his hip. Aligning them.

TOBY
Right. Absolutely. I could... I could run down to the lobby? Grab us some lattes while we map out the routing protocol?

VALERIE
No lattes. No protocols. I need those digits before Richard finishes his morning monologue and decides to audit the pending acquisitions.

Toby’s eyes dart toward the conference room door. He taps his folders again. Perfectly flush. A flawless ninety-degree angle.

TOBY
I-I-If I bypass the portal, it leaves a digital footprint.

VALERIE
Then wear lighter shoes.

Toby swallows hard.

TOBY
I’ll text it to you. Give me three minutes.

VALERIE
You have one.

Valerie turns on her heel. She strides down the corridor, her designer heels clicking against the hardwood like a ticking clock.

SMASH CUT TO:

scenes.narrative_action: Valerie stands before the sprawling, sterile modernist mansion, having successfully acquired the keys. A roaring, obnoxious sports car pulls up, and ARTHUR PENDELTON (40s) steps out. He completely ignores Valerie's greeting, loudly booming into his phone about a hostile takeover while dropping the name of his bespoke Italian tailor. Valerie swallows her pride, forces a tight, transactional smile, and gestures toward the imposing front door, waiting for him to finish.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: Introduces the sequence's victim and establishes his abhorrent personality to lessen the audience's empathy. Micro-Conflict (><): Valerie wants to control the showing and make her pitch vs. Arthur wanting to dominate the physical and auditory space. Value Shift (+/-): From prepared professionalism (+) to demeaned subservience (-).
scenes.draft_text:
EXT. MODERNIST MANSION - LATER

A sprawling, brutalist fortress of glass and black concrete.

VALERIE VANCE (30s) stands at the base of the geometric driveway. Her tailored suit is a flawless armor. She grips a heavy, brass key.

At her feet, a struggling cicada buzzes helplessly on its back against the scorching pavement.

Valerie crouches. With the tip of her pristine stiletto, she gently flips the insect upright. It scuttles into the manicured ivy.

A deafening, guttural ROAR shatters the quiet.

A neon-green McLaren 765LT whips into the driveway, tires screeching as it violently jumps the curb.

The gull-wing door swings up. ARTHUR PENDELTON (40s) steps out. He wears a custom linen suit that screams new money.

He is already shouting into a Bluetooth earpiece.

ARTHUR
I don't care if they cry, Greg! Gut the board. Liquidate the IP.

Valerie steps forward, deploying a high-wattage, transactional smile.

VALERIE
Arthur. Welcome to the pinnacle of architectural provenance. The spatial fluidity here is--

Arthur blows right past her, pacing the driveway. He takes up maximum physical space, waving his arms at the sky.

ARTHUR
(into earpiece)
Because I said so! Tell them my Caraceni tailor needs more closet space. Buy the whole block and bulldoze it!

Valerie’s smile tightens. She steps into his peripheral vision, holding up the brass key.

VALERIE
As you can see, the turnkey potential offers unmatched--

Arthur holds up a single, manicured finger directly in Valerie’s face. A silent command to shut up.

Valerie freezes. Her jaw clenches.

Her thumb drops to her wrist. She aggressively scrubs at an invisible smudge on her silk cuff.

Arthur finishes his call, tapping a complex, rhythmic Chopin arpeggio on the roof of his McLaren.

He finally looks at the house. He doesn't look at Valerie.

ARTHUR
Looks like a sterilized parking garage.

Valerie stops scrubbing her cuff. The mask slides perfectly back into place.

VALERIE
It’s a brutalist sanctuary. The poured-concrete aesthetic guarantees absolute privacy. A hard asset that insulates your portfolio from market volatility.

Arthur aggressively checks his heavy Audemars Piguet watch. He doesn't actually read the time.

ARTHUR
(booming)
I’ve got twenty minutes before I buy a yacht I don't even want. Impress me.

Valerie swallows the last ounce of her pride.

She extends a flat, subservient hand toward the monolithic front door. Waiting for him to lead.

VALERIE
After you.

Arthur marches past her, his heavy footsteps echoing toward the entrance.

CUT TO:

scenes.narrative_action: Valerie guides Arthur through the cavernous, sterile living spaces. She attempts to highlight the imported marble countertops, but Arthur constantly interrupts, loudly bragging about his own wealth and treating Valerie like a hired servant. As they walk, Valerie notices a stray, injured spider on the windowsill; she subtly scoops it up with a tissue and places it safely outside a cracked window, completely unnoticed by the booming tech bro.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: Demonstrates Valerie's paradox (saving the vulnerable bug) while escalating Arthur's obnoxious behavior to its peak. Micro-Conflict (><): Valerie tries to deliver her sanitized real estate pitch vs. Arthur aggressively talking over her. Value Shift (+/-): From attempting to guide the tour (+) to losing complete control of the narrative (-).
scenes.draft_text:
13 INT. MANSION FOYER / KITCHEN - CONTINUOUS

Footsteps echo like gunshots. The foyer is a cavern of glass and brutalist concrete. 

ARTHUR PENDELTON (30s) marches through the space. He wears a tailored suit that looks like it is fighting a losing battle against his broad, restless shoulders. 

VALERIE VANCE (40s) steps sharply behind him, her icy professional smile locked in place.

VALERIE
Notice the seamless transition from the entryway. The architect prioritized a curated flow, allowing the natural light to--

ARTHUR
(interrupting)
I need ceilings that breathe, Valerie. My place in Aspen? Vaulted. Thirty feet. You walk in, you feel small. I want people to walk in here and feel microscopic.

Valerie's smile tightens. She gestures toward the open-concept kitchen ahead.

VALERIE
The spatial volume here is unparalleled. If you will follow me to the chef's kitchen.

Arthur breezes past her, clipping her shoulder. He does not look back.

ARTHUR
Is there a wet bar? I am flying in the executive board from Loro Piana next week. They drink like sailors.

They enter the KITCHEN. A sterile expanse of imported black marble.

VALERIE
The countertops are single-slab Nero Marquina marble. Sourced directly from--

ARTHUR
Hey, do me a favor.

Arthur stops at the massive island. He taps the marble, unimpressed.

ARTHUR
Grab my assistant's bag from the Rover. I left my Montblanc pen in the side pocket. I cannot sign anything with whatever cheap plastic you are carrying.

Valerie freezes. Her eyes dart to the pristine marble. 

VALERIE
The paperwork is fully digital, Arthur. A bespoke biometric process.

ARTHUR
(booming laugh)
Digital! Right. Let me buy you a Macallan 25 later, teach you how real money works. Paper leaves a legacy.

He turns his back to her, pacing the length of the kitchen, inspecting the custom cabinetry.

Valerie exhales a slow, measured breath. Her gaze drifts to the windowsill.

A SPIDER. One of its legs is bent at a broken angle. It twitches helplessly against the glass.

ARTHUR
I mean, look at these cabinets. Are they even soft-close? I swear, if I hear a slam when I am hungover, I will fire the manufacturer myself.

Valerie pulls a silk tissue from her blazer pocket. She steps toward the window.

VALERIE
Custom acoustic dampening. You will not hear a thing.

With surgical precision, Valerie scoops the injured spider into the tissue. 

Arthur keeps talking, staring at the ceiling fixtures.

ARTHUR
Good. Because silence is expensive. You know what I paid for the soundproofing in my Maybach?

Valerie nudges a cracked window open. She gently taps the tissue. The spider tumbles onto the outside ledge, free.

VALERIE
I am sure the investment was substantial.

She closes the window, tucking the tissue away.

Arthur spins around, clapping his hands together. The sound violently echoes off the marble.

ARTHUR
Let's see the master suite. If the closet cannot fit two hundred pairs of limited-run Jordans, we are done here.

He marches out of the kitchen, completely taking over the tour. 

Valerie stands alone in the sterile kitchen for a beat. She obsessively scrubs at an invisible stain on her left cuff.

VALERIE
(to the empty room)
Right this way.

She follows his booming footsteps.

scenes.narrative_action: They move down a long, stark hallway. Valerie stops, blinking—the angles of the walls seem to subtly elongate and shift, creating an impossible, dizzying geometry. Arthur, entirely self-absorbed, marches straight ahead, completely ignoring the shifting architecture. Valerie shakes off the vertigo, blaming her stress, and hurries to catch up, desperate not to lose the massive commission.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: Injects the first overt supernatural element, transitioning the genre from corporate drama into horror. Micro-Conflict (><): Valerie's sensory perception warning her of danger vs. her desperate need to push forward and close the sale. Value Shift (+/-): From grounded reality (+) to eerie, supernatural disorientation (-).
scenes.draft_text:
INT. MANSION HALLWAY - CONTINUOUS

Arthur marches down the stark, brutalist corridor. His heavy footsteps boom against the polished concrete.

ARTHUR
It’s about volume, Val. Pure, unadulterated space. If I can’t fit the bespoke Fazioli grand in the atrium, what are we even doing? 

Valerie trails a few paces behind, her smile plastered firmly in place.

ARTHUR
I had my acoustician fly in from Milan for the Aspen property. Told him, look, if the resonant frequency shatters the Zalto glassware, that’s a you problem. Right?

Arthur doesn't look back. He simply expects an audience. 

Valerie opens her mouth to reply, but her breath catches. 

The vanishing point at the end of the hallway is receding. 

She stops walking. 

The symmetrical concrete slabs of the walls stretch forward like pulled taffy. A perfect ninety-degree ceiling joint slowly, silently bends into an impossible, dizzying obtuse angle.

Valerie grabs the cold wall. A wave of profound vertigo hits her.

ARTHUR
(echoing)
Are these floors radiant or just standard thermal mass? I don't do cold toes.

Valerie blinks hard. She looks down the corridor. 

Arthur is impossibly far away. A distance he could never have walked in five seconds. He is a small, oblivious figure marching into a geometric abyss.

Valerie’s breathing goes shallow. She vigorously rubs her thumb against the pristine cuff of her silk blouse, scrubbing a phantom stain into the expensive fabric. 

Her eyes dart back toward the safety of the foyer. The exit. 

Then, she looks back at Arthur. The walking commission. The only thing standing between her and absolute ruin.

Valerie drops her hand from her cuff. She forces the glossy, transactional smile back onto her face.

VALERIE
Bespoke hydronic loops! 

Her voice rings out, slightly too loud, echoing down the warped tunnel.

VALERIE
It’s all about... seamless environmental control! The architect really wanted to emphasize the forced perspective!

She pushes off the wall. 

Ignoring the sickening tilt of the floor, Valerie breaks into a rapid, clicking sprint to catch her client.

CUT TO:

scenes.narrative_action: Arthur steps aggressively into the cavernous master suite, still booming about his stock options. Before Valerie can cross the threshold, the heavy oak doors violently slam shut on their own, nearly crushing her fingers. Valerie grabs the heavy brass handles, pulling with all her body weight, but the doors are hermetically sealed, refusing to yield a single inch.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: Springs the trap, physically separating the protagonist from the victim. Micro-Conflict (><): Valerie uses all her physical strength to open the doors vs. the house's supernatural lockdown. Value Shift (+/-): From eerie disorientation (+) to absolute, adrenaline-fueled panic (-).
scenes.draft_text:
INT. MASTER SUITE THRESHOLD - CONTINUOUS

Arthur storms down the hallway, taking up all the oxygen.

ARTHUR
Liquidated the Vestiaire shares at the opening bell. If I'm parking fifty mil in this zip code, I need a bedroom that physically intimidates my guests.

Valerie trails him, her posture a flawless, icy spear.

VALERIE
The primary suite offers unparalleled spatial sovereignty. It is an aggressive footprint.

Arthur pivots, walking backward toward the cavernous, open oak doors of the master suite.

ARTHUR
Good. My board thinks I’m over-leveraged. I want them to walk in here and feel small.

He steps backward over the threshold.

VALERIE
You’ll find the acoustics are completely--

WHOOSH.

The heavy oak doors swing inward with bone-shattering velocity.

Valerie lunges.

SLAM.

The doors crash together. Valerie yanks her hand back. The wood grazes her knuckles. A fraction of an inch from amputating her fingers.

Silence rings in the hallway.

Valerie stares at the closed doors. Her professional mask slips for a microsecond before snapping back into place.

VALERIE
(steadying her voice)
Arthur. Apologies. The cross-breeze in these bespoke builds can be temperamental.

No answer.

She grabs the heavy brass handles. Pulls.

They do not budge.

VALERIE
Arthur? Just push the left panel.

ARTHUR (O.S.)
(muffled)
Hey! What kind of smart-home biometric garbage is this?

Valerie plants her designer heels against the hardwood floor. She yanks the brass handles, throwing her entire body weight backward.

The doors are hermetically sealed. Fused like a bank vault.

ARTHUR (O.S.)
(muffled, voice cracking)
Open the door! I have a hard out in ten minutes! Hey!

Valerie’s breathing turns ragged. She pulls until the tendons in her neck strain against her skin.

Nothing. Not a millimeter of give.

ARTHUR (O.S.)
(muffled, panicked)
What is that?

Valerie freezes. She presses her ear to the cold wood.

VALERIE
Arthur?

ARTHUR (O.S.)
Is the floor... moving? Hey! HEY!

A wet, sickening crunch echoes from behind the oak.

Then, dead silence.

Valerie stumbles back. Her chest heaves. Her eyes dart wildly across the pristine, unyielding wood grain.

She looks down at her expensive silk cuffs. Her breathing accelerates into a hyperventilating rhythm.

She begins to obsessively scrub at an invisible stain on her right sleeve.

CUT TO:

scenes.narrative_action: Trapped inside the shifting geometry of the master suite, Arthur's booming voice cracks in sudden, high-pitched panic. He obsessively checks his luxury watch, tapping the glass frantically without actually reading the time. Beneath his designer shoes, the pristine hardwood floor literally liquefies into a dark, viscous quicksand. In utter, terrifying silence, Arthur sinks into the floorboards, swallowed whole without a single drop of blood spilled.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: Delivers the sequence's major horror set-piece and executes the victim, establishing the monster's rules. Micro-Conflict (><): Arthur desperately tries to escape the liquid floor vs. the house actively consuming him. Value Shift (+/-): From loud, arrogant dominance (+) to silent, absolute erasure (-).
scenes.draft_text:
INT. MASTER SUITE - CONTINUOUS

The walls of the master suite groan. Not a settling house. A living exhale. 

The sharp, right angles of the modernist ceiling imperceptibly warp.

ARTHUR stands in the center of the vast, empty room. He scoffs, trying to project his usual volume.

ARTHUR
Valerie! If this is some smart-home acoustic trick, it's garbage. The acoustics in here are dead. I could barely hear my own Steinway!

No answer. The doorway behind him is gone. Replaced by a seamless expanse of Venetian plaster.

ARTHUR
I said cut the tech demo! I'm buying the place, alright? You can stop selling!

The geometry shifts again. The walls tilt inward by a fraction of a degree.

Arthur's breath hitches. His booming voice cracks into a sudden, high-pitched squeal.

ARTHUR
Hey! Open the door!

He looks at his wrist. A platinum Patek Philippe. He taps the sapphire glass frantically. Tap. Tap. Tap. 

He stares at the dial, eyes wide, processing absolutely nothing about the time.

ARTHUR
Valerie! I'll buy you a bottle of... of the '82 Cheval Blanc! Just open the damn door!

He takes a step forward.

SQUELCH.

Arthur looks down. His bespoke Berluti loafer doesn't rest on the pristine Brazilian walnut floorboards. It rests in them.

The wood grain swirls. It liquefies. 

A dark, viscous quicksand replaces the solid floor beneath his feet.

ARTHUR
What the--

He yanks his leg up. The thick, tar-like substance clings to his calf, pulling him deeper. 

He loses his balance and crashes to his knees.

The liquid floor ripples, hungry. It rises to his waist in seconds.

ARTHUR
(high-pitched, breathless)
Help! Somebody! Do you know who I am?! I'm Arthur Pendelton! I own half the grid in this zip code!

He claws at the solid floor a few feet away. His manicured fingernails scrape uselessly against the dry wood.

The house makes no sound. No mechanical grind. Just a terrifying, absolute silence.

The dark liquid reaches his chest. 

Arthur taps his watch again. A desperate, involuntary spasm. Tap. Tap.

ARTHUR
(cracking whisper)
Please. I'll pay... anything.

The floor surges upward, cresting over his shoulders.

He throws his head back, mouth open for one final, booming scream.

The liquid wood rushes into his throat. It chokes the sound instantly.

He sinks. The top of his head slips beneath the surface. 

A single bubble rises and pops.

The liquid darkens, hardens, and instantly cures back into flawless Brazilian walnut.

Not a single drop of blood stains the wood. The master suite sits perfectly empty. Perfectly silent.

scenes.scene_heading: INT. MANSION HALLWAY / MASTER SUITE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.narrative_action: The brass handles suddenly click. Valerie stumbles forward as the heavy oak doors swing open effortlessly. She bursts into the master suite, panting, only to find it completely empty. The floorboards have solidified into perfect, pristine alignment. A heavy, flawless diamond rolls across the smooth wood, coming to a dead stop right at the tip of Valerie's heels.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: Reveals the aftermath of the monster's meal and introduces the supernatural reward. Micro-Conflict (><): Valerie searches frantically for her client vs. the impossible, sterile emptiness of the room. Value Shift (+/-): From frantic rescue mode (-) to stunned, paralyzed disbelief (+).
scenes.draft_text:
INT. MANSION HALLWAY / MASTER SUITE - CONTINUOUS

*Click.*

The heavy brass handles of the master doors snap down in perfect unison.

The resistance vanishes. VALERIE stumbles forward, her shoulder slamming into the thick oak. The doors swing inward with a silent, frictionless glide.

She bursts into the cavernous master suite, chest heaving, eyes wild.

VALERIE
Arthur!

Silence. The acoustics of the room swallow her voice.

VALERIE
(panting)
Mr. Pendelton! The seller is-- we can negotiate the escrow! Just come out!

She spins, heels skidding on the hardwood. She tears back the heavy velvet curtains. She throws open the custom walk-in closet.

Nothing.

The suite is impossibly, entirely empty.

Valerie drops to her knees. Her palms slap against the floorboards. She presses her face close to the wood, her manicured nails scraping at the seams.

The gaps are gone.

The oak planks have solidified into a seamless, pristine expanse of woodwork. No trapdoors. No structural anomalies. Not a single drop of blood or a scuff mark from Arthur's oversized Italian loafers.

The room smells faintly of fresh pine and ozone.

VALERIE
(breathless)
This is a load-bearing floor. You can't just...

She pushes herself back. Her icy veneer shatters completely.

Her hands tremble violently. She stares at her wrists, eyes locking onto the cuffs of her expensive silk blouse. She begins to scrub frantically at the fabric. There is no stain, but she digs her thumb in, rubbing obsessively, her breathing escalating into a ragged hyperventilation.

VALERIE
(muttering)
Where is the client. Where is my commission...

A sharp, heavy *CLACK*.

Valerie freezes. Her thumb stops tearing at her cuff.

From the far, shadowed corner of the suite, a small object drops onto the floorboards.

*Clack. Clink. Rrrrrrroll.*

It catches the ambient sunlight, refracting a blinding, prismatic spectrum across the sterile walls.

Valerie stays on her knees, paralyzed, tracking its movement.

The object rolls smoothly across the perfect wood. It loses momentum, wobbling slightly, before coming to a dead stop.

Right at the tip of Valerie's leather heel.

A raw, uncut diamond. The size of a plum.

Flawless.

Valerie stares down at the stone. Her frantic breathing stops entirely.

SMASH CUT TO:

scenes.narrative_action: Valerie drops to her knees, her trembling fingers brushing the cold, heavy facets of the impossible gem. Suddenly, her phone violently vibrates in her pocket. She pulls it out to see a flashing, final foreclosure warning on her own luxury condo. She stares at the exact spot where Arthur was digested, the sheer terror of the supernatural execution clashing violently against the blinding promise of absolute financial salvation.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: Forces the protagonist into the central moral debate of the film. Micro-Conflict (><): The terrifying reality of the murder vs. the life-saving financial value of the diamond. Value Shift (+/-): From paralyzed shock (-) to agonizing, high-stakes temptation (+).
scenes.draft_text:
INT. MASTER SUITE - CONTINUOUS

The house settles. A deep, structural groan echoes through the HVAC vents, followed by dead silence.

No blood. No torn clothes. Arthur Pendelton is simply gone.

Valerie’s knees hit the polished concrete floor.

Her chest heaves in shallow, jagged gasps. She stares at the empty space in the center of the room.

Resting on the floorboards, exactly where Arthur stood, is a raw diamond.

It is the size of a golf ball.

Valerie’s hands shake violently. She aggressively scrubs at the cuff of her silk blouse. There is no stain. She scrubs harder, friction burning her wrist.

She crawls forward.

Her trembling fingers hover over the stone. She brushes the facets.

It is freezing. Impossibly heavy.

She pulls her hand back.

She grabs her phone. She opens the keypad.

VALERIE
(hyperventilating)
Nine... one...

She stops. Her thumb hovers over the final digit.

The phone VIOLENTLY vibrates in her palm.

An alert flashes across the screen. Red text.

FINAL NOTICE OF FORECLOSURE. PROPERTY SEIZURE IN 24 HOURS.

Valerie stares at the words. The color drains from her face. She traces the edge of the screen, her breathing completely stopping.

She looks back at the empty space. Just the scuff marks from Arthur's shoes.

Then, she looks at the diamond.

She deletes the dialed numbers.

She taps a new contact. Puts the phone on speaker.

VALERIE
Toby. Cancel the afternoon showings.

TOBY (V.O.)
(phone filter)
Cancel them? Valerie, Arthur Pendelton's team is asking for the closing disclosures--

VALERIE
Mr. Pendelton backed out. He found the open-concept layout... suffocating.

Valerie reaches out. She wraps her fingers around the diamond.

TOBY (V.O.)
(phone filter)
He walked away? But your commission--

VALERIE
We are pivoting, Toby. The demographic for this property just shifted. Exclusivity is paramount.

She lifts the diamond into the sunlight. Brilliant, blinding prisms fracture across her pale face.

VALERIE
We need high-net-worth buyers. Only the most aggressive portfolios.

TOBY (V.O.)
(phone filter)
Valerie, are you okay? You sound out of breath.

Valerie stands up. The terror in her eyes hardens into an icy, unblinking stare.

She drops the heavy stone into her blazer pocket.

VALERIE
I have never been better. The market is finally correcting itself.

She ends the call.

She smooths the wrinkles from her skirt, turns her back on the execution spot, and walks out of the suite.

scenes.narrative_action: Valerie hovers her thumb over her phone's emergency keypad, 9-1 already typed out. The crushing weight of her impending bankruptcy bears down on her. Her flawless mask slips entirely; she begins obsessively scrubbing at invisible stains on her expensive cuffs, her breathing ragged. Slowly, she deletes the numbers. She slips the massive gem into her designer pocket, coldly pivoting her attention to the magnificent architectural crown molding of the ceiling as she silently accepts the house's dark terms.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: Cements Valerie's dark pact with the entity, ending the sequence on a point of no return. Micro-Conflict (><): Valerie's fading moral conscience wants to report the crime vs. her insatiable greed demanding she keep the gem. Value Shift (+/-): From agonizing indecision (-) to cold, calculated, and corrupted resolve (+).
scenes.draft_text:
INT. MASTER SUITE - MOMENTS LATER

Dead silence. 

The sheer scale of the room swallows VALERIE. She stands frozen near the center of the pristine, untouched hardwood floor.

In her trembling hand: a smartphone.

The screen glares with harsh white light. The emergency dialer is open. 

9 - 1 -

Her thumb hovers over the final '1'. Shaking.

A banner notification drops down across the top of the screen:
CITIBANK ALERT: ACCOUNT OVERDRAWN. FINAL NOTICE OF DEFAULT.

Valerie’s chest heaves. The perfectly constructed ice-queen facade shatters entirely.

She gasps for air, a dry, ragged sound echoing off the vaulted ceiling. 

Her free hand snaps to her left wrist. Her fingers claw at the edge of her expensive silk cuff.

She scrubs the fabric. Hard. Frantic. 

There is no stain. There is nothing there at all. 

She keeps scrubbing anyway, her manicured nails digging into her own skin through the silk. The crushing weight of her reality caves in on her.

The house settles. A low, resonant creak in the floorboards reverberates through the soles of her designer heels. 

It sounds almost expectant. Patient.

Valerie looks down.

Resting perfectly in the center of a geometric sunbeam on the floor is the diamond.

Flawless. Massive. 

Not a single drop of Arthur’s blood taints its facets.

Valerie stops scrubbing her cuff. 

Her breathing slows. The manic terror in her eyes hardens into something cold. Calculating.

She kneels. 

Her fingers close around the gemstone. It is heavy. 

She stands. 

She looks down at her phone screen. The emergency numbers still pulse.

Tap. The '1' vanishes.

Tap. The '9' vanishes.

She locks the screen. 

Valerie slips the massive diamond into the pocket of her tailored blazer. 

She turns her back to the empty space where Arthur was just consumed. 

Her eyes drift upward, tracing the sweeping lines of the room. 

She studies the magnificent, hand-carved architectural crown molding. 

Her posture straightens. The icy mask slides flawlessly back into place. 

She nods once to the empty room. A silent, binding contract.

SMASH CUT TO:

scenes.narrative_action: Valerie steps out the heavy front door, pulling it shut until the deadbolt clicks with a heavy, final thud. She stands on the porch, her hand shoved deep into her designer pocket, fingers tightly gripping the blood-gem. The sky opens up, rain slicking the driveway. She pulls her coat tight, her eyes hardening as she turns her back on the house and marches toward the street, leaving the monster behind.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: Transitions Valerie from the supernatural trauma of the climax into the physical reality of the city. Micro-Conflict: Valerie vs. the lingering shock of the murder. Value Shift: (-) Traumatized to (+) Resolute.
scenes.draft_text:
EXT. MODERNIST MANSION - DAY

The heavy oak front door of the sprawling estate swings open. VALERIE stumbles out onto the concrete threshold.

Her usually immaculate designer suit is rumpled. Her breathing is shallow, erratic. She stares back into the dark, silent maw of the foyer.

She grabs the massive brushed-steel handle. Pulls it shut.

The deadbolt engages with a heavy, final THUD.

Valerie stands frozen on the expansive porch. The silence of the property is deafening. Her hands tremble violently.

She looks down at her expensive silk cuffs. Frantically, she scrubs at an invisible stain on the fabric. She digs her manicured nails into the material, trying to erase the invisible horror.

She forces herself to stop. Her chest heaves. She drops her hand, plunging it deep into her coat pocket.

Inside the dark fabric, her fingers curl. Her knuckles turn white as she tightly grips the jagged edges of the blood-gem.

The sharp facets bite into her palm. A grounding, profitable pain.

A low rumble of thunder echoes overhead. The gray sky cracks open.

Raindrops the size of coins smack against the slate driveway. The sudden downpour slicks the minimalist landscaping.

Water streaks down Valerie's face, plastering her hair to her cheeks. She pulls the lapels of her coat tight against her chest.

The violent trembling in her shoulders finally subsides. Her jaw sets. Her panicked eyes harden into cold, calculating glass.

She turns her back on the concrete monster.

Valerie marches down the slick driveway toward the street. Her designer heels click with a ruthless, steady rhythm through the rain.

She does not look back.

CUT TO:

scenes.narrative_action: Rain lashes the pavement. Valerie marches through the gray downpour, clutching her designer bag tight against her chest like a physical shield. She weaves through the bustling crowd, ignoring the glittering, brightly lit storefronts of the main avenue, and ducks into a narrow, dimly lit alleyway approaching a nondescript security door.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: Physically crossing the threshold into the illicit underworld. Micro-Conflict: Valerie vs. the elements and her own vulnerability. Value Shift: (-) Exposed to (+) Hidden.
scenes.draft_text:
EXT. DIAMOND DISTRICT - DAY

Rain lashes the gray pavement in violent, icy sheets.

VALERIE VANCE powers through the deluge. No umbrella. Her soaked designer trench coat clings to her shivering frame.

She clutches her leather Birkin bag tight against her chest. A ten-thousand-dollar physical shield against a city that wants to swallow her whole.

A sea of black umbrellas forces her toward the rushing gutter. A BUSINESSMAN violently bumps her shoulder, nearly sending her into the muddy street.

BUSINESSMAN
Watch the shoes, sweetheart!

Valerie stumbles, her expensive heels skidding on the slick concrete. She doesn't snap back. She just tightens her death grip on the bag. Vulnerable. Drowning in plain sight.

To her left, a gauntlet of brightly lit jewelry storefronts display pristine, glittering diamonds. Warmth. Safety. The legitimate world.

Valerie stares at the glowing windows for a fractured second. Her jaw tightens. She doesn't belong there anymore.

She turns her back on the light.

Valerie ducks into a narrow, dimly lit alleyway. The roar of the city traffic instantly muffles into a hollow, dripping echo.

Trash lines the brick walls. The shadows deepen.

She marches to the dead end. A rusted, nondescript steel security door blocks her path. No handle. Just a glowing red intercom lens.

Valerie wipes the freezing rain from her eyes. Under the flickering security light, she obsessively scrubs at an invisible stain on her soaked French cuffs.

She presses the buzzer.

SMASH CUT TO:

scenes.scene_heading: INT. JEWELRY BROKER - BACK ROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.narrative_action: Behind a reinforced steel door, a grim-faced appraiser sits under a harsh halo light. He slides his jeweler's loupe over the impossible facets of the blood-gem. His cynical sneer melts into absolute, greedy awe. Valerie stands across from him, her posture rigid. Her usual desperate tremor is gone. She slaps her hand on the table and demands a heavy briefcase of untraceable cash.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: Validates the house's currency, cementing the dark pact. Micro-Conflict: Valerie wants maximum value vs. the appraiser's inherent skepticism. Value Shift: (-) Awaiting judgment to (+) Financially empowered.
scenes.draft_text:
INT. JEWELRY BROKER - BACK ROOM - CONTINUOUS

A deadbolt THUNKS. The reinforced steel door seals shut, trapping the stale air inside.

Under the sterile glare of a harsh halo light, an APPRAISER sits at a velvet-lined table. His face is carved from cheap cigar smoke and decades of cynicism. 

Across from him stands VALERIE. Her posture is a steel rod. The desperate, vibrating tremor that usually haunts her hands is completely gone. 

The Appraiser pinches a massive, pristine blood-gem between titanium tweezers. He slides a jeweler's loupe over his right eye.

APPRAISER
Lab-grown. Has to be. Mother Nature doesn't bake them this clean.

VALERIE
Consider it a new development. Zero previous tenants. The foundation is flawless.

He grunts, angling the stone under the halo lamp.

APPRAISER
Everyone says their heirloom is flawless. Usually, it's just polished glass and delusion.

He leans closer. The cynical sneer holding his face together begins to slacken. 

Inside the gem, the light shatters into impossible, blinding facets. 

The sneer melts. Absolute, naked, greedy awe takes its place. He lowers the tweezers, his breathing suddenly shallow.

APPRAISER
(quiet)
No inclusions. No carbon spots. Not a single fracture. Where exactly did you source this?

VALERIE
A highly motivated seller. Completely off-market.

APPRAISER
It's too clean. Bloodless. Stones this size usually leave a trail of bodies. I'll need provenance before I can write a check.

VALERIE
I specialize in clean properties. What's the appraisal?

He rolls the diamond onto the velvet. He cannot take his eyes off it.

APPRAISER
I can wire you two hundred thousand. But the paperwork takes three days.

Valerie steps into the light. She SLAPS her bare hand flat onto the table. The tweezers jump.

VALERIE
We're bypassing escrow. I need immediate liquidity. Five hundred.

APPRAISER
Five? For an undocumented rock?

VALERIE
In a heavy briefcase. Untraceable cash.

APPRAISER
You're out of your mind.

VALERIE
Look at the light return. You know exactly what the comps are for a turnkey asset like this in a private showing. I am offering you the ground floor. Take it, or I walk this listing across the street to a more aggressive buyer.

The Appraiser stares at her hand, then back to the diamond. The silence stretches, thick and heavy. 

He pulls the loupe from his eye. The greed has entirely swallowed him.

APPRAISER
I'll have to make a call. The vault doesn't keep that much paper on hand.

Valerie does not blink. She just taps her manicured fingernail against the velvet.

VALERIE
Make the call.

SMASH CUT TO:

scenes.narrative_action: The heavy thud of banded hundred-dollar bills hits a polished mahogany desk. Valerie slides her foreclosure notice across the surface. The bank manager stares at the cash, stunned, before picking up his stamp and bringing it down hard, marking it paid in full. Valerie exhales, the crushing weight of her financial ruin evaporating. She stands up, her posture rigidly straight, and leaves without a backward glance.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: Eliminates Valerie's immediate survival stakes, shifting her motivation from desperation to greed. Micro-Conflict: Valerie wants her freedom vs. the bank's bureaucratic authority. Value Shift: (-) Drowning in debt to (+) Liberated.
scenes.draft_text:
INT. MORTGAGE LENDER'S OFFICE - DAY

LYLE (50s), a bank manager in a suit that costs more than a car, leans back in his chair. He steeples his fingers. 

LYLE
--it’s not a personal failing, Valerie. The market corrects. It breathes. And sometimes, over-leveraged agents get exhaled. We’re simply reclaiming the asset.

VALERIE
It’s my childhood home, Lyle.

LYLE
It’s a collateralized debt obligation. And as of midnight tonight, the property defaults to--

THWACK.

Six thick bricks of banded hundred-dollar bills hit the polished mahogany.

Valerie slides a bright red FORECLOSURE NOTICE across the wood. 

VALERIE
Count it.

Lyle freezes. The bureaucratic smugness wipes clean off his face. He stares at the mountain of cash.

LYLE
We... we don't process physical currency for mortgage arrears. It’s a compliance nightmare. 

VALERIE
Highly liquid. Zero friction. It’s exactly three hundred and twelve thousand. Plus your predatory late fees.

Lyle pokes the top stack. Like it might bite him. 

LYLE
Valerie. Where did you get this? The IRS requires a Form 8300. I have to log the exact source of the funds.

Valerie stares at the surface of his desk. 

VALERIE
Is that mahogany veneer?

LYLE
What?

VALERIE
Your desk. It looks like solid wood, but the grain repeats right... there. Veneer peels when it gets too dry. 

LYLE
The source of the funds, Ms. Vance.

VALERIE
An exclusive listing. Off-market. The buyer bypassed escrow for an expedited closing.

LYLE
In cash?

VALERIE
He appreciates tangible assets.

Lyle looks back at the money. It is so much money. The strict walls of banking bureaucracy waver in the face of raw, undeniable capital. 

LYLE
I'll... have to get the compliance officer to sign off--

VALERIE
You'll get your stamp.

Silence. The HVAC hums overhead. Valerie’s icy, unblinking gaze pins him to his chair. 

Lyle swallows hard. He opens his top drawer. He pulls out a heavy, mechanical stamp. 

KA-CHUNK.

Over the red 'NOTICE OF FORECLOSURE', bold blue ink bleeds into the paper: PAID IN FULL.

Valerie stares at the blue ink. 

Her rigid shoulders drop a fraction of an inch. A silent, shuddering exhale escapes her nose. The invisible anvil lifts off her chest. The crushing weight of her entire life evaporates.

She stands. Her spine perfectly straight. 

LYLE
Valerie, if you're suddenly holding this kind of capital... our wealth management division could--

VALERIE
The desk is peeling, Lyle. Moisturize it.

She turns on her heel and strides out the glass doors. She never looks back.

scenes.narrative_action: Inside a sterile, ultra-modern fitting room, Valerie slips into a razor-sharp, tailored crimson designer suit. She stares at her reflection in the full-length mirror, adjusting the lapels, recognizing the ruthless predator staring back. She pulls out her phone, opens her contacts, and deliberately bypasses a list of desperate, working-class families. She scrolls directly to the city's worst corporate offenders, tapping a name with a manicured nail.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: The Break into Two moment; Valerie fully commits to her new role as the monster's middleman. Micro-Conflict: Valerie's lingering conscience vs. her newfound appetite for power. Value Shift: (+) Relieved survivor to (+) Ruthless predator.
scenes.draft_text:
INT. LUXURY BOUTIQUE - DAY

Stark, shadowless lighting. Mirrors at every geometric angle. A sterile, ultra-modern fitting room that feels more like a surgical theater.

VALERIE VANCE stands in the dead center.

She slides her arms into a razor-sharp, tailored crimson designer suit. 

She pulls the fabric taut. It fits like a second skin. Armor for a new tax bracket.

On the floating glass bench, her phone BUZZES.

Valerie doesn't look away from the mirror. She reaches down blindly and taps the speaker icon.

TOBY (V.O.)
(filtered)
Valerie? The bank rejected the duplex extension. And the Martinez family is waiting in the lobby.

Valerie steps closer to the glass. She adjusts the rigid lapels.

VALERIE
The duplex lacks fundamental structural integrity, Toby. It’s a depreciating asset. We’re moving on.

TOBY (V.O.)
(filtered)
They have the deposit in cash. They've been sleeping in a Honda Civic for six days.

Valerie freezes. 

Her eyes dart from her reflection to the phone. The words hit a buried nerve.

She looks back at the mirror. The crimson suit makes her look drenched in fresh blood. 

Her breathing shallows. Her right hand spasms. 

She obsessively scrubs at the pristine cuff of her sleeve. Digging fiercely at a stain that doesn't exist.

VALERIE
Physical cash is a liability in this quarter. It lacks pedigree.

TOBY (V.O.)
(filtered)
Valerie, please. I promised them. They just need a roof--

VALERIE
Cancel the lower-tier showings. We are pivoting our portfolio.

She stops scrubbing the cuff. Her posture straightens. 

VALERIE
Exclusivity is our new baseline.

She taps the screen. The call dies.

Absolute silence returns to the white room. 

Valerie stares at her reflection. The panic is gone, replaced by a cold, dead calm. 

She recognizes the ruthless predator staring back. She likes her.

Valerie picks up the phone. Opens her contacts.

Her manicured thumb scrolls.

Past Martinez, Family.

Past Miller, Desperate.

Past O'Connor, Eviction Notice.

She bypasses the working-class pleas without a second glance.

The scrolling accelerates. A blur of names. 

She crosses into the corporate heavyweights. The sharks.

Her thumb stops abruptly.

Sterling Corp - Hostile Acquisitions.

Valerie smiles. A sharp, hollow cut across her face.

She taps the name with a perfectly filed nail.

SMASH CUT TO:

scenes.narrative_action: Valerie struts through the glass double doors of the agency, her new crimson armor radiating an aura of untouchable success. Richard Sterling is leaning against a cubicle, mid-sentence in a predatory lecture to a junior agent. He stops, his smirk faltering as he takes in Valerie's transformation. Valerie does not even break stride. She completely ignores his presence, cutting right through his personal fiefdom and marching straight toward Toby's desk.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: Re-establishes the office power dynamic and positions Valerie as a threat to Richard. Micro-Conflict: Valerie wants to assert dominance vs. Richard's established authority. Value Shift: (-) Subservient employee to (+) Untouchable rival.
scenes.draft_text:
INT. CORPORATE BULLPEN - DAY

The frosted glass double doors swing open. 

VALERIE VANCE steps through. She wears a tailored crimson suit. Armor.

The ambient hum of ringing phones and clacking keyboards instantly dies down.

At a center cubicle, RICHARD STERLING leans heavily over a terrified JUNIOR AGENT.

RICHARD
The market isn't a spreadsheet, Kevin. It's a canopy. The saplings at the bottom don't get sunlight. They rot into the soil so the redwoods can touch the sky. Are you soil, Kevin?

Richard spots the flash of crimson. His predatory smirk evaporates.

He stands straight. Valerie is already moving. Her heels click against the linoleum in a steady, metronomic rhythm.

She charts a path directly through his personal fiefdom.

RICHARD
Valerie. We need to discuss the--

She doesn't break stride. Doesn't even blink.

Richard steps into the aisle, blocking her trajectory. He leans in close, invading her personal space.

RICHARD
(low, through clenched teeth)
You don't ghost my calls. A forest requires a food chain.

VALERIE
Move the ficus by the water cooler, Richard. The foliage is asymmetrical. It depreciates the aesthetic value of the entire floor plan.

She sidesteps him without breaking eye contact with the far wall. 

Richard is left standing in the aisle, his jaw tight.

Valerie arrives at a cramped desk in the corner.

TOBY HIGGINS is frantically aligning his stapler, tape dispenser, and coffee mug into perfect ninety-degree angles.

TOBY
M-Ms. Vance. I wasn't expecting-- Did the closing on the modernist property finalize? Is Mr. Pendelton... satisfied?

VALERIE
The client has been permanently relocated.

Valerie drops a heavy, leather-bound folder onto his perfectly aligned desk.

VALERIE
Clear your afternoon. We are pivoting to an exclusive, invite-only model. High-yield assets only.

TOBY
But my starter-home viewings-- The young couple from--

VALERIE
Cancel them. The neighborhood's comps are garbage anyway.

Toby stares at the heavy folder. He reaches out, adjusting it a fraction of an inch so it sits perfectly parallel to his keyboard.

SMASH CUT TO:

scenes.scene_heading: INT. CORPORATE BULLPEN - TOBY'S DESK - CONTINUOUS
scenes.narrative_action: Valerie drops a sleek, embossed leather folder squarely onto Toby's perfectly aligned paperwork, shattering his quiet concentration. She leans in, tasking him with curating a highly exclusive VIP list of corrupt politicians and toxic executives. Toby blinks behind his glasses. He pushes the folder back, arguing earnestly that these individuals represent the exact opposite of the community-building ethos they are supposed to champion.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: Introduces the B-Story moral counterweight. Micro-Conflict: Valerie wants a hit-list vs. Toby's earnest moral compass. Value Shift: (+) Dominant to (-) Resisted by an ally.
scenes.draft_text:
INT. CORPORATE BULLPEN - TOBY'S DESK - CONTINUOUS

Toby traces a complex structural anomaly on a sprawling architectural blueprint. 

A sleek, embossed leather folder SLAPS down, dead center on the schematic. 

Valerie towers over the desk. Icy. Immaculate.

VALERIE
I need a curation. Top tier only. The kind of buyers who don't blink at eight figures and possess highly aggressive capital.

Toby blinks behind his glasses. He gently pushes the folder a quarter-inch to perfectly align with the edge of his keyboard.

TOBY
Aggressive? Ms. Vance, the brief said we were targeting philanthropic anchors. For the neighborhood.

VALERIE
Philanthropy doesn't close by Friday. I want Sterling's private Rolodex. The zoning commissioners. The offshore tech acquisitions guys.

TOBY
Wait. You mean Arthur Pendelton's circle? Half of them are actively displacing low-income tenants in the Arts District. 

VALERIE
They are highly motivated.

TOBY
It completely violates the community-building ethos the firm outlined in the Q3 manifesto. 

Valerie stares at him. She reaches out, her manicured nail tracing the gold embossing on the leather folder.

VALERIE
Do you know what Italian calfskin costs, Toby? It breathes. It has memory. It doesn't care about a manifesto. It just holds what has value.

Toby's hands twitch. He squares off a stack of neon Post-its, aligning them into a perfect ninety-degree angle.

TOBY
I-I could go grab your flat white? The espresso machine in the lobby is finally fixed.

VALERIE
I don't want coffee. I want the names. 

TOBY
They're toxic. Two of those commissioners are under federal investigation.

VALERIE
Which means they require a private sanctuary. Immediately. 

Valerie leans in. The professional mask remains flawless, but her thumb furiously rubs at an invisible blemish on her pristine French cuff. 

VALERIE (CONT'D)
Compile the list. Or I find an assistant who understands market realities.

Toby freezes. The threat hangs in the fluorescent light. He looks down at the blueprint trapped beneath the leather folder.

TOBY
The floorplan for the master suite... the load-bearing walls don't even make structural sense.

VALERIE
Focus on the names, Toby.

She turns and strides down the aisle. 

Toby stares at the leather folder like it might bite him.

CUT TO:

scenes.scene_heading: INT. CORPORATE BULLPEN - TOBY'S DESK - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.narrative_action: Valerie pivots seamlessly. She lowers her voice, framing the initiative as a wealth redistribution marketing campaign designed specifically to overcharge the corrupt. Toby's shoulders relax. Under the mild cognitive dissonance, he develops a slight stutter, compulsively re-organizing the leather folder into a perfect right angle with his keyboard. He nods, eagerly accepting the deceptive mission.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: Valerie actively corrupts her only ally, securing the bait for the house. Micro-Conflict: Valerie's manipulation vs. Toby's lingering doubts. Value Shift: (-) Facing resistance to (+) Securing compliance.
scenes.draft_text:
INT. CORPORATE BULLPEN - TOBY'S DESK - MOMENTS LATER

Toby stares at a freshly printed guest list. His finger hovers over the bolded names, trembling slightly.

TOBY
I just... I need to clarify the objective here, Valerie. These are Richard's private equity contacts. They don't buy homes, they buy zip codes. Is this really our demographic?

Valerie leans over the low cubicle partition. She checks over her shoulder, then drops her voice to a conspiratorial whisper.

VALERIE
It’s a wealth redistribution marketing campaign, Toby.

Toby blinks. 

TOBY
A... redistribution?

VALERIE
Think of the macro-level optics. Who are these men? The exact predators who price young families out of the market. We aren't serving them. We are strategically overcharging the deeply corrupt.

Toby’s tense shoulders drop. A long breath escapes him. 

The moral math twists visibly behind his eyes. He reaches for a leather presentation folder resting on his desk.

TOBY
But the- the liability. If Richard finds out we intercepted his- his prime buyers...

VALERIE
Look at the stitching on that folder.

Toby stops. He looks down at his hands.

VALERIE
Hand-tooled Italian calfskin. Richard expensed those the same week he froze your overtime pay. We are simply leveraging their bloated liquidity to fund our own independence.

Toby swallows hard. 

He slides the leather folder to the right. He aligns its bottom edge perfectly flush with his ergonomic keyboard. 

A flawless ninety-degree angle.

TOBY
So... we're just acting as an aggressive market correction?

VALERIE
We are balancing the ledger.

Toby taps the corner of the folder. Once. Twice. The cognitive dissonance settles in.

TOBY
I- I can draft the NDAs. And the exclusive invitations. I'll use the heavy cardstock.

VALERIE
Make them feel untouchable.

Toby nods, a frantic, eager rhythm taking over.

TOBY
Right. Yes. Untouchable. I'll- I'll get the courier scheduled immediately.

Valerie steps back from the cubicle. Her icy professional veneer slides perfectly back into place.

CUT TO:

scenes.narrative_action: Valerie sits in her glass-walled office, sipping an espresso. Through the glass, she watches Toby diligently color-coding the dossiers of the city's most despicable elites. The frantic, supernatural horror of the mansion feels miles away, muted by the rhythmic tapping of Toby's keyboard. She leans back in her expensive chair, her isolated guilt transforming into a twisted sense of collaborative camaraderie as she enjoys the calming breather.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: Concludes the sequence by grounding the horror in a deceptive, mundane office environment. Micro-Conflict: Valerie's internal guilt vs. the comfort of her new reality. Value Shift: (-) Isolated and guilty to (+) Connected and calm.
scenes.draft_text:
INT. VALERIE'S OFFICE - LATER

Sterile, soundproof glass separates the inner sanctum from the bullpen.

VALERIE sits in her ergonomic leather throne. The delicate ceramic clink of her espresso cup echoes in the quiet room.

Through the glass wall, TOBY hunches over his desk. He meticulously attaches neon-red sticky tabs to a towering stack of thick client dossiers.

Valerie watches him. Her reflection in the glass overlays his earnest, oblivious face.

She presses the blinking intercom button on her console.

VALERIE
The red tabs, Toby. Walk me through the demographic.

Toby jumps slightly, looking up at the glass. He leans into his desk mic.

TOBY
(filtered)
Oh. Right. The red ones are our Tier-One aggressive accumulators. Should I cross-reference their offshore holdings?

Valerie exhales. The rigid tension in her shoulders melts. She leans back, taking a slow, steady sip of espresso.

VALERIE
Just their liquidity.

TOBY
(filtered)
Massive. But they're... well, they're the demographic that yells at valets. Notoriously difficult to satisfy.

Valerie traces the rim of her cup. A faint, dark smile touches her lips.

VALERIE
They’ll find the new property highly accommodating. Fast-track the red tabs for Sunday’s private showing.

TOBY
(filtered)
Absolutely. It's a seller's market, right?

Toby offers a cheerful smile through the glass.

Valerie doesn't return it. She just watches him resume typing. The rhythmic, muffled clatter of his keyboard bleeds through the speaker, drowning out the silence.

She closes her eyes. Safe. Insulated.

CUT TO:

scenes.narrative_action: Valerie finishes her espresso, sets the delicate cup down on her desk, and steps out of her glass-walled office. She approaches Toby's desk, physically pulling the stack of color-coded dossiers out of his hands. She flips to the top file--a notoriously corrupt city councilman--taps the man's photo decisively, and grabs her designer coat, leaving Toby to manage the phones.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: Transitions Valerie from passive observation to active predation, officially beginning her dark pact. Micro-Conflict (><): Valerie wants to immediately initiate the feedings vs. Toby wanting to carefully review the files. Value Shift (+/-): From (+) Calm isolation to (+) Predatory momentum.
scenes.draft_text:
INT. VALERIE'S OFFICE - CONTINUOUS

Valerie drains the last drop of espresso. 

She lowers the delicate porcelain cup to her pristine desk. 

Through the glass walls of her office, Toby is visible at his workstation in the bullpen. 

Valerie pushes through the heavy glass door. 

Toby sits behind a barricade of color-coded dossiers. He holds a thick red folder, tracing a ruler down a printed spreadsheet.

VALERIE
Give me the high-yield acquisitions. 

TOBY
(startled)
I’m still running the background matrices. If we’re matching clients to the modernist listing, we need to be careful.

He squares the edges of the red folder with the edge of his desk. Perfect ninety-degree angle.

TOBY
Councilman Graves is at the top, but his public record is... complicated. He has three pending lawsuits for tenant intimidation.

VALERIE
A robust legal history suggests a man who appreciates aggressive negotiations.

TOBY
I really should cross-reference his escrow history. And I was looking at the mansion's floor plans--there’s a structural anomaly in the sub-basement I can't quite--

Valerie steps into his personal space. 

She reaches down and physically pulls the stack of dossiers right out of his hands.

TOBY
M-Ms. Vance, the disclosures--

VALERIE
The modernist property requires a specific caliber of occupant, Toby. Someone with a massive footprint.

She flips open the top folder. 

A glossy headshot of COUNCILMAN GRAVES stares back. Slick hair, predatory smile.

Valerie taps the councilman’s face with a lacquered fingernail. Twice. Hard.

VALERIE
He’s hungry for a legacy. We’re going to feed that ambition.

She pivots, snatching her camel-hair designer coat off the brass rack.

VALERIE
Manage the phones. If Gemma Frost calls, tell her I’m showing a private sanctuary.

TOBY
Should I prep the standard liability waivers?

Valerie shrugs into her coat.

VALERIE
Don't waste the paper. 

She strides toward the elevator, heels clicking like a metronome against the hardwood. 

Toby watches her go, absently aligning his stapler with his tape dispenser.

CUT TO:

scenes.narrative_action: The corrupt City Councilman paces beneath the soaring vaulted ceiling, aggressively jabbing his finger in the air as he loudly demands a bribe disguised as a broker's fee. Valerie maintains a flawless, icy professional smile and deliberately steps backward into the adjoining hallway. Above the Councilman, the massive crystal chandelier violently detaches. The heavy glass and iron crush him instantly against the marble floor. No blood spills. A flawless, fist-sized ruby rolls from beneath the wreckage. Valerie casually steps forward and scoops it up.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: Executes the first deliberate murder for profit, proving the house's mechanics. Micro-Conflict (><): The Councilman wants to extort Valerie vs. Valerie waiting for the house to execute him. Value Shift (+/-): From (-) Extorted to (+) Wealthy and victorious.
scenes.draft_text:
INT. MANSION - GRAND FOYER - DAY

Vaulted ceilings stretch into the stratosphere. Dead center hangs a massive, tiered crystal chandelier. It looks less like a light fixture and more like a suspended glacier. 

COUNCILMAN CARMICHAEL (50s, sweating through a bespoke wool suit) paces the imported marble floor. He jabs a thick finger at the air.

VALERIE VANCE stands near the archway. Hands clasped. Her smile is a flawless, icy barricade. 

CARMICHAEL
Let’s not insult each other’s intelligence, Valerie. An exclusive zoning variance for a property of this footprint? It requires oversight. Heavy oversight.

VALERIE
The structural integrity speaks for itself. The permits were filed perfectly.

CARMICHAEL
Permits are paper. I deal in reality. And the reality is, my office requires a localized broker's consultation fee. Paid upfront. In cash.

Valerie’s eyes dart to the floor. A struggling honeybee spins on its back against the baseboard, a torn wing fluttering. 

She smoothly crouches, scooping the bee onto the edge of a glossy property brochure. 

CARMICHAEL
Are you listening to me? I can tie this listing up in environmental reviews until the next ice age. 

Valerie gently deposits the bee onto the broad leaf of a potted monstera plant. She stands, brushing a microscopic speck of dust from her cuff.

VALERIE
I hear your concerns, Councilman. But I strongly encourage you to evaluate the aesthetics of the space before we discuss penalties. 

She takes a deliberate step backward, toward the adjoining hallway. 

VALERIE
(gesturing upward)
For instance. The verticality of this foyer. It demands a specific kind of presence to truly appreciate the craftsmanship.

Carmichael scoffs, stepping forward to close the distance. He steps directly onto the intricate compass rose inlaid in the center of the marble floor. 

Directly beneath the chandelier.

CARMICHAEL
Stop pitching me! You think you have the leverage to stall? I will bury you. I will bury this house. Fifty thousand. Today. Or I padlock the gates.

Valerie takes one more step back. Her heels click against the hardwood of the hall. She is completely out of the foyer.

VALERIE
The house evaluates all offers.

Above Carmichael, a sound echoes like a submarine hull groaning under extreme pressure. 

He looks up. 

The heavy iron chain holding the chandelier SNAPS. 

Two tons of Bohemian glass and forged iron drop in a free-fall. 

It hits Carmichael with the force of an asteroid. 

The impact shakes the foundation. A deafening, concussive CRUNCH of metal and marble. 

A cloud of white plaster dust billows outward. 

Valerie doesn't flinch. She doesn't blink. She just watches the dust settle. 

Beneath the tangled wreckage of iron and shattered crystal, there is no body. No pulverized flesh. Not a single drop of blood. 

Total silence. 

From beneath a twisted brass candelabra, something catches the ambient sunlight. 

It rolls across the intact marble. Clinking softly. 

It stops inches from Valerie’s designer heel. 

A flawless, fist-sized ruby. Glowing with a deep, impossible fire. 

Valerie’s icy smile finally reaches her eyes. 

She casually crouches, scoops the heavy gemstone into her palm, and drops it into her blazer pocket.

CUT TO:

scenes.scene_heading: INT. HIGH-END JEWELER / LUXURY BOUTIQUE - DAY
scenes.narrative_action: Valerie drops the massive ruby onto a jeweler's velvet tray. The appraiser's eyes widen in shock. Moments later, Valerie strides through a high-end boutique, dropping a stack of shopping bags onto a pristine glass counter. She swipes a sleek black card, purchasing a diamond-encrusted luxury watch. She checks her reflection in the mirror, her posture radiating absolute arrogance.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: Establishes the immediate, intoxicating financial reward of her dark pact. Micro-Conflict (><): Valerie wants to consume luxury vs. the lingering psychological shadow of her past poverty. Value Shift (+/-): From (+) Wealthy to (++) Untouchable.
scenes.draft_text:
INT. HIGH-END JEWELER - DAY

A flawless, blood-red RUBY hits a black velvet tray. Heavy. Dense.

The APPRAISER lowers his jeweler’s loupe. His hands tremble. 

APPRAISER
(hushed)
The clarity... I’ve never seen a stone with zero inclusions. Where did you source this?

Valerie does not blink. Her posture is a steel rod.

VALERIE
It’s a distressed asset. Turnkey condition. Let’s focus on the liquidity.

APPRAISER
I can wire the funds immediately, but a piece this aggressive... it requires authorization.

VALERIE
Make the call.

SMASH CUT TO:

INT. LUXURY BOUTIQUE - LATER

Six glossy shopping bags slam onto a pristine glass counter.

Valerie stands before a floor-to-ceiling mirror. She wears a sharply tailored blazer that screams new money. 

A CLERK slides a velvet box across the glass. Inside rests a diamond-encrusted chronograph watch.

CLERK
Our flagship timepiece. Though, I should mention, it is quite an investment.

Valerie stares at her reflection. Her eyes drop to her sleeve. Her fingers twitch, compulsively rubbing at an invisible, phantom stain on her pristine cuff. 

She grabs the heavy, blinding watch. Straps it directly over the cuff. The twitching stops. 

VALERIE
Appreciate the architecture, don't worry about the zoning. I'll take it.

She taps a sleek titanium black card against the payment terminal. A digital chime rings out. Approved.

Valerie meets her own eyes in the mirror. Her chin tilts up. Untouchable.

CUT TO:

scenes.narrative_action: Gemma Frost aggressively critiques the mansion's open-concept layout, her voice echoing with passive-aggressive jealousy. As Gemma ascends the floating glass staircase, Valerie watches silently from the foyer below. Suddenly, the architectural geometry of the stairs flattens, transforming the steps into a frictionless, vertical slide. Gemma shrieks, clawing at the glass as she plunges backward into a dark, expanding void beneath the floorboards. The floor seals shut. Valerie walks over and retrieves a stunning sapphire from the base of the stairs.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: Eliminates Valerie's primary rival and escalates the house's power to manipulate its architecture. Micro-Conflict (><): Gemma wants to belittle the property to assert dominance vs. Valerie letting the house defend its value. Value Shift (+/-): From (-) Annoyed by rival to (+) Triumphant over rival.
scenes.draft_text:
INT. MANSION - FLOATING STAIRCASE - DAY

Sunlight bleaches the foyer. The centerpiece of the room is a FLOATING GLASS STAIRCASE, a jagged spine of transparent treads suspended in mid-air. 

GEMMA FROST (30s, draped in aggressively loud Chanel) circles the base of the stairs. She taps her phone screen, checking her reflection in the darkened glass, adjusting a stray blonde curl. 

VALERIE VANCE (30s, tailored, icy) stands a few feet away, perfectly still.

GEMMA
It’s bold, Val. Truly. It takes a certain... desperate bravery to list a property with absolutely zero privacy. 

VALERIE
The open-concept sightlines maximize natural light. It’s an architectural statement. 

GEMMA
It’s a glass cage, sweetie. Who is the target demographic here? Exhibitionists with offshore accounts?

VALERIE
The seller requires a buyer who appreciates seamless environmental integration. 

Gemma lets out a bubbly, hollow laugh. She slides a manicured finger over the pristine glass banister. 

GEMMA
Seamless. Right. Just like the escrow on the Meyer estate. You integrated yourself right into my commission on that one, didn't you? 

VALERIE
(smooth)
The client wanted a more aggressive portfolio strategy. I simply facilitated the transition. 

Valerie’s thumb finds the edge of her silk cuff. She rubs aggressively at an invisible smudge. 

GEMMA
You stole it. Which is fine, it’s business, but let’s not pretend you’re curating fine art here. I know your pipeline is bone dry. 

Gemma steps onto the first glass tread. 

A low, subsonic HUM vibrates through the floorboards. 

Gemma pauses. Her thumb moves to her index finger, her nail digging sharply into her own cuticle. 

GEMMA
Is the floor supposed to vibrate? Or did your inspector miss a foundational flaw? 

VALERIE
Radiant sub-floor heating. It runs through the core framework. High-yield efficiency. 

Gemma ascends to the third step. The fifth. She looks down at Valerie, her fake smile dropping into something sharp and predatory. 

GEMMA
I could throw you a lifeline, you know. Co-list it with me. Let someone with actual liquidity handle the marketing before you completely embarrass yourself. 

VALERIE
The seller demands absolute exclusivity. A co-list would dilute the brand prestige. 

GEMMA
Brand prestige? 

Gemma picks at her cuticle harder. A bead of crimson blood wells up on her skin. 

GEMMA
Valerie, you’re wearing last season’s Prada and trying to sell a transparent coffin. Arthur Pendelton already vanished from your ledger. You are drowning. 

Valerie steps back. She looks up at Gemma, her icy veneer locking into place. 

VALERIE
Mr. Pendelton was absorbed by the property's unique ecosystem. The listing remains highly competitive. 

GEMMA
(scoffs)
You actually believe your own pitch. It's pathetic--

SHIFT. 

The architectural geometry of the staircase snaps. 

The horizontal glass treads instantly flatten, angling downward. The entire staircase transforms into a sheer, frictionless vertical slide. 

Gemma SHRIEKS. 

Her designer heels slip. She plunges backward. 

Her manicured nails claw frantically at the smooth glass, screeching like chalk on a blackboard, finding zero purchase. 

At the base of the stairs, the hardwood floorboards unhinge. They yawn open to reveal a pitch-black, expanding VOID. 

Gemma drops into the darkness. 

Her scream echoes, stretching into an unnatural, hollow distortion. 

SLAM. 

The floorboards snap shut, seamlessly locking together. 

The staircase folds back into perfect, horizontal steps. 

Silence returns to the sunlit foyer. 

Valerie stands perfectly still. Her chest rises and falls in a slow, controlled breath. She smooths her silk cuffs, her hands steady. 

She walks to the base of the stairs. 

Resting perfectly centered on the pristine hardwood, exactly where the void had opened, is a massive, flawless SAPPHIRE. 

Valerie kneels. She picks it up. 

The gemstone catches the sunlight, casting a cold blue fracture across her face. 

CUT TO:

scenes.narrative_action: A kinetic, rapid-fire sequence. Valerie practically dances through the mansion's corridors in a series of new designer outfits. She leads a parade of embezzlers, slumlords, and toxic CEOs into different rooms. The house devours them in creative traps: a floorboard opens like a maw, a wall constricts to crush a slumlord. Valerie gleefully collects handfuls of impossible gemstones, stuffing them into her designer pockets, completely ignoring the horrific reality of the slaughter.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: Delivers the 'Fun and Games' promise of the premise, showcasing the monster's creative kills and Valerie's unchecked greed.
