Micro-Conflict (><): The terrible clients want to evaluate the house vs. the house actively eating them. Value Shift (+/-): From (+) Triumphant to (++) Drunk on power.
scenes.draft_text:
INT. MANSION - VARIOUS ROOMS - DAY

A kinetic, high-fashion blur. VALERIE struts through the sun-drenched corridors. 

She wears a brand-new, cobalt-blue Armani suit. 

Trailing her is a HEDGE FUND BRO, glued to his phone.

HEDGE FUND BRO
I need aggressive square footage. Something that says 'I own the zoning board.'

VALERIE
The open-concept flow here is entirely uncompromising. It demands attention.

HEDGE FUND BRO
It feels a little... hollow.

He steps onto a rich mahogany floorboard. 

The wood splinters. The floor unhinges like a massive, geometric jaw. 

HEDGE FUND BRO
Wait, what the--

The floor snaps shut. He vanishes. Not a single drop of blood.

Valerie doesn't even blink. 

A loud, sharp clink. 

A flawless, two-carat diamond rests on the polished wood. 

Valerie scoops it up. She drops it into her pocket and keeps walking.

INT. MANSION - NARROW HALLWAY - MOMENTS LATER

Valerie wears a crimson Dior trench coat. 

She leads a SWEATY SLUMLORD down the corridor. He taps the pristine plaster with a gold-tipped cane.

SWEATY SLUMLORD
Load-bearing walls? I usually gut these. Cram in six micro-units. Maximize the yield.

VALERIE
This property is highly adaptive to its occupants' needs.

SWEATY SLUMLORD
I'll tear it down to the studs.

The hallway groans. 

Valerie casually steps forward, crossing the threshold into the living room. 

Behind her, the hallway walls violently slam together. A sickening, muffled crunch.

Valerie checks her reflection in the foyer mirror. She adjusts her lapel.

The walls slowly retract to their normal width. 

Where the Slumlord stood, a cluster of heavy rubies clatters to the floor. 

Valerie sweeps them into her designer clutch.

INT. MANSION - MASTER SUITE - LATER

Valerie, now draped in an emerald velvet blazer, gestures to a cavernous walk-in closet. 

A NEUROTIC EMBEZZLER clutches a metal briefcase to his chest.

NEUROTIC EMBEZZLER
Offshore is dead. I need tangible assets. Concrete. Something the feds can't subpoena.

VALERIE
The privacy here is absolute. It is an impenetrable vault.

Valerie pauses. She notices a small, injured moth fluttering frantically against the window pane. 

She approaches the glass. Gently, she cups the moth in her manicured hands. 

She carries it to a potted fern and sets it down safely on a leaf.

The Embezzler steps into the closet.

NEUROTIC EMBEZZLER
Is there a hidden safe?

The closet doors elongate. The cedar shelving morphs, stretching outward to wrap around him like a wooden python.

NEUROTIC EMBEZZLER (CONT'D)
Hey! The door is stuck!

The wood constricts. His screams are instantly silenced. 

Valerie turns her back to the closet, admiring the vaulted ceilings.

A torrent of pristine sapphires spills out from under the closet door. 

They pool at Valerie's expensive heels. 

She kneels. Her pockets bulge, heavy and misshapen with impossible wealth. 

She scoops the sapphires up by the handful, a manic, breathless smile stretching across her face. 

She is practically vibrating. Untouchable.

SMASH CUT TO:

scenes.narrative_action: Valerie pulls up to the mansion in a brand-new, idling luxury sedan. Toby sits in the driver's seat, dutifully reviewing paperwork. Valerie steps out of the back, escorting a notoriously vile Hedge-Fund Manager toward the front doors. She gives Toby a dismissive hand gesture to stay in the car, eager to secure her largest diamond yet without his interference.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: Sets up the midpoint trap and isolates Toby as the innocent bystander outside the house's domain. Micro-Conflict (><): Valerie wants to rush the next kill vs. the Manager's slow, arrogant pace. Value Shift (+/-): From (++) Drunk on power to (+) Highly expectant.
scenes.draft_text:
EXT. MANSION - DRIVEWAY - DAY

Tires crunch over crushed white gravel. A gleaming, dealership-fresh obsidian Maybach rolls to a halt before the looming modernist mansion. 

TOBY sits in the driver's seat. He ignores the luxury interior, his eyes locked on a sprawling architectural blueprint draped across the steering wheel. He taps a silver pen, aligning it perfectly parallel with the leather dashboard stitching.

The rear door swings open. VALERIE steps out. Her tailored suit is sharper than a scalpel. 

She turns back, offering a manicured hand to the cavernous backseat. 

BRENT (50s, fleece vest over a bespoke suit) emerges. He moves with the agonizingly slow, gelatinous pace of a man who has never been told to hurry. 

BRENT
(stretching loudly)
Cell reception dies a mile down the canyon. Unacceptable. 

VALERIE
Total privacy is the ultimate luxury asset, Brent. The brutalist symmetry acts as a natural Faraday cage. You are completely insulated from the noise.

Brent pauses. He casually crushes a blossoming orchid near the walkway under his Italian loafer, scraping dirt off his sole.

BRENT
Insulated. Right. Tell that to the SEC. 

Toby rolls down the driver's window. He holds up the blueprint, his brow furrowed. 

TOBY
Ms. Vance? I was reviewing the county topological surveys. The sub-basement dimensions here, they... they don't actually align with the structural foundation. There's a massive void space.

Valerie's jaw tightens. She flashes Toby a blinding, frozen smile.

VALERIE
Value-add potential, Toby. 

TOBY
But the load-bearing--

VALERIE
Stay in the car. Organize the escrow files. 

She gives a sharp, dismissive flick of her wrist. A master commanding a dog. Toby shrinks back, rolling the window up. 

Valerie pivots to Brent, gesturing toward the towering, windowless front doors. 

VALERIE
Shall we? The interior flow is aggressively open-concept. 

Brent doesn't move. He pulls out his phone, squinting at a downloaded spreadsheet. 

BRENT
Hang on. If the pension fund defaults, we absorb the collateral. Let the retirees eat cake. 

Valerie stares at the heavy front doors. A low, subsonic hum vibrates through the gravel. The house is waiting. 

Valerie’s thumb aggressively rubs at an invisible smudge on her silk cuff. 

VALERIE
(voice tightening)
The imported obsidian entryway. Notice the seamless integration. 

BRENT
(still looking at phone)
I said hang on, Valerie. I'm liquidating a textile plant. 

Valerie steps directly into Brent's personal space. Her eyes are dead, but her voice is pure velvet. 

VALERIE
Brent. The market waits for no one. And this property has a voracious appetite. 

Brent finally looks up from his screen. He smirks, entirely misreading her intensity. 

BRENT
Voracious. I like that. 

He pockets the phone and lumbers up the concrete steps. 

Valerie follows, her eyes locked onto the back of his neck. 

The heavy doors glide open on their own. The pitch-black foyer swallows them both. 

The doors slam shut. 

In the Maybach, Toby flinches at the sound. He looks back down at the blueprints, tracing the impossible void space with a trembling finger.

scenes.narrative_action: Valerie escorts the Hedge-Fund Manager into the cavernous dining room. The arrogant financier barks loudly into his cell phone, ignoring Valerie entirely. He aggressively kicks a pristine baseboard with his expensive loafer, scuffing the paint. Valerie steps back into the doorway, crossing her arms and smiling expectantly, waiting for the house to consume him.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: Triggers the house's rejection mechanism by presenting a corrupted soul. Micro-Conflict (><): The Manager wants to disrespect the property vs. Valerie waiting for his execution. Value Shift (+/-): From (+) Highly expectant to (-) Confused.
scenes.draft_text:
INT. MANSION - DINING ROOM - DAY

Footsteps echo like gunshots. 

VALERIE VANCE leads a HEDGE-FUND MANAGER across a cavernous dining room. Pristine white oak floors. Floor-to-ceiling glass. Absolute architectural perfection.

The Manager presses a phone to his ear. He paces, completely oblivious to the cathedral-like elegance. 

HEDGE-FUND MANAGER
(into phone)
I don't care if they strike. Liquidate the pension block. Bleed it out.

Valerie glides alongside him. Her icy professional veneer is locked firmly in place.

VALERIE
You'll notice the organic flow of the floor plan. The architect prioritized a seamless transition between the living spaces. It is an entertainer's absolute paradise.

HEDGE-FUND MANAGER
(into phone)
Tell legal to stall. We starve them into taking the settlement.

VALERIE
The acoustics alone add a seven-figure premium. 

The Manager snaps his phone shut. He glares at the vast, empty room. 

HEDGE-FUND MANAGER
It's sterile. Feels like a mausoleum.

He swings his foot. His heavy, custom Italian loafer slams into the pristine baseboard. 

A harsh black scuff marks the flawless white paint.

Valerie stops dead. Her eyes lock onto the scuff mark. 

She doesn't flinch. She doesn't reprimand him. 

Instead, she takes a slow, measured step backward. She glides over the threshold, retreating into the safety of the doorway. 

Valerie crosses her arms. A serene, chilling smile creeps across her face. 

She waits.

The Manager sighs, aggressively scrolling through his emails. 

HEDGE-FUND MANAGER
And the natural light is offensive. I'd have to tint these windows.

Beneath his feet, the white oak floorboards groan. 

A low, guttural vibration hums through the walls. The modernist chandelier above him begins to tremble. The crystal clinking sounds like grinding teeth.

Valerie's smile widens. Her eyes gleam with anticipation. 

The shadows in the corners of the room stretch, creeping toward the Manager's ankles. The floor beneath his loafers softens, dipping slightly like a hungry throat preparing to swallow.

HEDGE-FUND MANAGER
(tapping his phone)
Zero reception in here, too. Unacceptable.

The vibration reaches a fever pitch. The floorboards part by a fraction of an inch. 

Then--

A wet, violent shudder echoes through the plumbing. It sounds exactly like a suppressed gag. 

The vibration instantly dies. 

The shadows snap back to the corners. The floorboards harden. The chandelier goes perfectly still.

Dead silence.

Valerie's smile falters. Her arms drop to her sides. 

She stares at the floor. Confused. 

HEDGE-FUND MANAGER
You know what? I'm bored. Show me the wine cellar.

He brushes past Valerie, stepping out of the dining room without a single scratch. 

Valerie remains frozen in the doorway. She looks from the scuffed baseboard to the perfectly solid floor. 

VALERIE
(whispering)
What is wrong with you?

She isn't looking at the Manager. She is glaring at the house.

scenes.narrative_action: The floorboards ripple aggressively beneath the Manager's feet. Instead of opening into a dark void, the hardwood floor bucks violently. The floorboards act like a massive springboard, launching the screaming billionaire backward through the air, completely rejecting him as a meal.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: Subverts Valerie's expectations and changes the rules of the monster's appetite. Micro-Conflict (><): Valerie wants the house to eat the billionaire vs. the house violently refusing the meal. Value Shift (+/-): From (-) Confused to (--) Shocked.
scenes.draft_text:
INT. MANSION - DINING ROOM - CONTINUOUS

Valerie stands rigid by the archway. Her eyes lock onto the intricate parquet floor beneath Arthur's Italian loafers.

ARTHUR
I’m telling you, Val, the acoustics are garbage. I’d have to gut this entire wing just to fit the Steinway.

Valerie holds her breath. Waiting for the drop.

VALERIE
The structural integrity is the property's strongest asset, Arthur.

A deep, guttural hum vibrates through the walls. The crystal chandelier shivers.

Arthur checks his gold Patek Philippe. A bead of sweat drips down his temple.

ARTHUR
Is there a subway out here?

The floorboards beneath his feet ripple. Not a sinkhole. A fluid, rolling wave.

Valerie steps back. Her icy veneer fractures. This is wrong.

The hardwood bows deeply downward, groaning under immense, unnatural tension.

Then--

SNAP.

The floorboards buck violently upward. A massive, kinetic springboard.

Arthur SCREAMS.

He is launched backward through the air, completely airborne.

He crashes hard into the custom mahogany dining table. Wood splinters.

Arthur groans, a broken heap among the scattered silver.

Valerie stares at the floor. The parquet snaps back into a seamless, pristine pattern.

Total rejection. 

Valerie aggressively scrubs a thumb over her pristine silk cuff. 

ARTHUR
(wheezing)
What the hell is wrong with this place?!

Valerie ignores him, her eyes glued to the immovable floor.

VALERIE
(whispering)
Why aren't you closing?

SMASH CUT TO:

scenes.narrative_action: The Hedge-Fund Manager flies out the front doors and crashes hard onto the concrete driveway. He scrambles to his feet, bruised and terrified, and sprints down the street without looking back. In the idling sedan, Toby watches the man run away, his brow furrowing in deep confusion as he looks toward the open front doors.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: Physically removes the corrupted prey from the board, leaving the house hungry. Micro-Conflict (><): The Manager wants to escape the entity vs. gravity and the concrete. Value Shift (+/-): From (--) Shocked to (---) Alarmed.
scenes.draft_text:
EXT. MANSION - DRIVEWAY - CONTINUOUS

The massive oak front doors of the mansion EXPLODE outward.

A HEDGE-FUND MANAGER is violently ejected into the daylight. He sails over the porch steps, limbs flailing.

He slams hard onto the concrete driveway. A sickening scrape of expensive wool and bruising flesh.

He doesn't even check his bleeding elbows. Pure, unadulterated terror overrides the impact. 

Scrambling like a cornered animal, the Manager finds his footing. He sprints blindly down the manicured street, abandoning his dignity and his loafers. He never looks back.

In an idling sedan parked near the curb, TOBY sits frozen.

His hands grip the steering wheel. He watches the fleeing millionaire shrink into a speck down the road.

Toby’s breath hitches. His brow furrows in deep, paralyzing confusion. 

He slowly shifts his gaze back to the gaping, pitch-black maw of the mansion's open front doors. 

Trembling, Toby reaches blindly toward the stack of glossy property brochures on the passenger seat.

He doesn't take his eyes off the dark entrance. His shaking fingers slide the brochures across the upholstery, meticulously aligning their edges into a flawless, ninety-degree right angle against the center console. 

The house waits.

SMASH CUT TO:

scenes.narrative_action: The heavy oak front doors slam shut with a thunderous crack. The deadbolts slide into place with terrifying finality, locking Valerie inside. The mansion's walls begin to pulse with a low, rhythmic vibration that rattles her teeth. The dining room windows shatter inward simultaneously, showering the hardwood floor in jagged glass.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: Traps the protagonist and escalates the stakes from a financial game to an existential threat. Micro-Conflict (><): Valerie wants to maintain control of the environment vs. the house physically trapping her. Value Shift (+/-): From (---) Alarmed to (----) Trapped and panicked.
scenes.draft_text:
INT. MANSION - GRAND FOYER - CONTINUOUS

The heavy oak front doors SLAM shut. A thunderous crack swallows all other sound in the cavernous space.

Click. Clack. THUNK.

Brass deadbolts slide into their strike plates with terrifying finality. No human hands touch them.

Valerie spins. Her pristine posture instantly fractures. She lunges for the ornate iron handles.

She yanks them. Solid, unyielding resistance.

VALERIE
(breathless)
Faulty smart-home integration. Just a simple firmware glitch.

She throws her entire body weight against the oak. Her designer heels slip on the polished Italian marble. The door refuses to yield a single millimeter.

Valerie digs into her blazer pocket and retrieves her phone. 

Zero bars. "Searching for Network."

VALERIE
You have got to be kidding me. This is a prime coverage zone.

A low, bass-heavy hum originates from deep beneath the floorboards. 

The pristine, minimalist walls begin to pulse. It is a rhythmic, sickening vibration that travels straight up through Valerie's stilettos.

Her jaw tightens. Her teeth violently rattle against each other.

She backs away from the front doors, her eyes darting across the flawless architecture. The house feels alive. Breathing. 

Her thumb finds the edge of her expensive silk cuff. She scrubs at an invisible stain. Frantic. Obsessive.

VALERIE
(voice trembling)
It's just minor structural settling. Environmental acoustics. We can sanitize that in the disclosure packet.

The rhythmic vibration swells. The hum drops an octave, vibrating in her chest like a predatory growl.

Valerie looks past the grand staircase, toward the adjacent formal dining room.

The massive, floor-to-ceiling custom windows groan. The reinforced glass bows inward, warping the afternoon sunlight.

VALERIE
No, no, no. The replacement cost on those--

The dining room windows SHATTER simultaneously.

A deafening explosion of jagged glass erupts into the house. A tidal wave of razor-sharp shards showers across the imported hardwood floor.

Valerie screams, throwing her arms over her head. She drops to the marble floor as crystal shrapnel rains down, tearing through the fabric of her blazer.

The violent noise fades into the gentle tinkling of settling glass.

Valerie slowly lowers her arms. She is surrounded by a glittering sea of debris. 

The walls hum with a low, satisfied purr. She is sealed inside.

SMASH CUT TO:

scenes.narrative_action: Valerie sprints to the front doors, frantically yanking at the brass handles, but the house refuses to yield. Through the reinforced glass, she spots Toby waiting patiently in the idling sedan. The pulsing walls suddenly cease. The deadbolts click open in a synchronized, inviting rhythm. Realizing the house is demanding Toby's pure soul for its next meal, Valerie's mask of icy professionalism completely shatters. She backs away from the door, obsessively scrubbing at an invisible stain on her expensive cuffs, consumed by absolute dread.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: Delivers the midpoint revelation that the house demands an innocent soul, stripping Valerie of her control and forcing a moral crisis. Micro-Conflict (><): Valerie wants to escape without sacrificing Toby vs. the house holding her hostage until she feeds it his soul. Value Shift (+/-): From (----) Trapped and panicked to (-----) Profoundly doomed.
scenes.draft_text:
INT. MANSION - ENTRYWAY - CONTINUOUS

Valerie sprints across the Italian marble foyer. Her designer heels skid. 

She crashes into the massive custom double doors. Seizes the brushed brass handles. Yanks. 

Solid iron resistance. 

The walls hum. A low, rhythmic vibration pulses behind the pristine white drywall. The house is digesting.

Valerie slams her shoulder against the reinforced frosted glass. 

VALERIE
(under her breath)
Open the damn premium egress.

Through the frosted pane, a blurred silhouette: TOBY (20s) sits in the idling sedan. Oblivious. 

A frantic, sharp buzzing interrupts her panic. 

A large honeybee batters itself against the lower pane of the door, trapped inside the house. 

Valerie freezes. Her chest heaves. She drops to her knees, gently cupping the exhausted bee in her trembling palms. She deposits it safely onto the broad leaves of a nearby potted Ficus. 

She pulls her phone. Dials. 

Through the glass, Toby answers his cell. 

TOBY (V.O.)
(filtered)
Valerie? Did Mr. Pendelton execute the addendum?

VALERIE
We are pivoting the strategy, Toby. The buyer... withdrew his physical presence.

TOBY (V.O.)
(filtered)
He left? Without the structural inspection? Should I draft a cancellation of escrow?

VALERIE
Just put the car in drive. I am exiting the property.

Valerie grips the brass handle again. Pulls with her entire body weight. 

Nothing. The walls throb. The floorboards vibrate against her soles.

TOBY (V.O.)
(filtered)
Are you locked in? The smart-glass looks opaque. That usually indicates a localized power failure or a structural wiring anomaly.

VALERIE
It is a bespoke security feature. Top of the line. 

TOBY (V.O.)
(filtered)
I can come in and check the primary breaker.

VALERIE
No. Stay in the vehicle. Protect the asset's curb appeal.

Through the glass, Toby drops his phone from his ear. He unbuckles his seatbelt. 

VALERIE
Toby. Do not--

Toby steps out of the sedan. He stands on the driveway, the picture of earnest concern. 

The instant his foot touches the concrete, the walls stop vibrating. 

The low hum dies. Dead silence. 

CLICK. 

A heavy deadbolt slides back. 

CLICK. CLICK. 

Three more locks disengage in a synchronized, inviting rhythm. 

The massive doors drift open by an inch. A cool breeze whistles through the gap. 

Valerie stares at the crack of daylight. 

Through the opening, Toby approaches the porch. 

TOBY
(muffled)
Valerie? You got it open?

She looks at the open door. Then at Toby's pure, eager face. 

The house did not yield to let her out. It opened to let him in. 

Valerie backs away from the threshold. Her pristine posture crumbles. 

She retreats into the shadows of the foyer. Her eyes lock onto her wrists. 

Her manicured nails dig into her expensive silk cuffs. She scrubs. Hard. Clawing frantically at an invisible stain that will not come out. 

Toby's footsteps echo on the porch steps. 

CUT TO:

scenes.narrative_action: Valerie stands frozen as the mansion's heavy brass doors click open on their own. Toby steps onto the threshold, earnestly smiling and holding a coffee tray, completely oblivious to the floorboards humming with predatory anticipation beneath him. Valerie lunges forward, physically blocking the doorway. She violently snatches the coffee from Toby's hands and barks a harsh, transactional command for him to return to the office and file non-existent escrow paperwork immediately. Startled by her icy aggression, Toby retreats to the sedan. Valerie slams the heavy doors shut, locking the monster inside with her.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: Resolves the immediate cliffhanger of the previous sequence while establishing the house's growing, uncontrollable hunger. Micro-Conflict (><): Valerie wants to forcefully eject Toby to save his life vs. Toby wanting to enter the house to assist her. Value Shift (+/-): From paralyzed terror (-) to temporary, breathless relief (+).
scenes.draft_text:
INT. MANSION - ENTRYWAY - CONTINUOUS

Valerie stands paralyzed. 

The heavy brass front doors CLICK unlatched. They swing inward on their own.

TOBY stands on the sunlit threshold. He beams, balancing a cardboard tray of iced coffees. 

Beneath his wingtip shoes, the Brazilian walnut floorboards hum. A low, rhythmic vibration. Predatory. 

Dust motes dance above the wood grain, gravitating toward his ankles.

TOBY
I got oat milk for the--

Valerie lunges. She slams her body into the doorframe, physically barricading the entrance.

She snatches the cardboard tray from his hands. Coffee sloshes over the rims, burning her knuckles. She ignores it.

VALERIE
(breathless)
The Pendleton escrow. 

TOBY
The... sorry? I thought you needed help staging the--

VALERIE
Did I ask for staging? The Pendleton escrow is in freefall, Toby. The buyer's agent is threatening to pull earnest money. 

Toby blinks, his earnest smile melting into panic. He compulsively straightens his tie, aligning the knot perfectly with his collar buttons.

TOBY
B-but the disclosures cleared yesterday. I filed them myself. 

The humming beneath them grows louder. The mahogany paneling beside Valerie groans, shifting a fraction of an inch. 

Valerie’s thumb obsessively digs into the silk cuff of her blouse, scrubbing at an invisible stain.

VALERIE
Then you filed them wrong. 

TOBY
I-- I can come inside and pull up the portal on your iPad--

He takes a half-step forward. 

A floorboard snaps upward right at the threshold. A wooden jaw unhinging.

VALERIE
Get in your car. 

Toby freezes. 

VALERIE
Drive back to the office. Pull the physical addendums. Audit the entire chain of title. If I lose this commission because you don't know the difference between a contingency and a covenant, you're done in this industry.

Toby shrinks. The absolute ice in her voice crushes him. 

TOBY
I'll... I'll fix it. Right now.

He backs away, retreating down the flagstone path toward the parked sedan. 

Valerie watches him go. Her chest heaves.

She grips the brass handles and hauls the heavy doors shut. 

The deadbolt SLAMS home. 

Silence. Just the low, hungry purr of the house surrounding her.

scenes.narrative_action: Valerie is on her hands and knees, aggressively scrubbing a phantom stain off the pristine hardwood floor. She is surrounded by a bucket of bleach and discarded rags. The floorboards beneath her begin a rhythmic, impatient thrumming that shakes the soapy water in the bucket. Unnerved by the entity refusing to wait for a scheduled feeding, Valerie drops her sponge and grabs the brass doorknob to leave. The heavy mahogany door violently slams shut on its own, ripping the knob from her grip. The deadbolt clicks into place.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: Shifts the house from a passive trap for clients into an active, aggressive threat toward Valerie herself. Micro-Conflict (><): Valerie wants to clean up and leave the premises vs. the house demanding immediate sustenance. Value Shift (+/-): From a false sense of managerial control (+) to sudden physical entrapment (-).
scenes.draft_text:
INT. MANSION - MASTER BEDROOM - LATER

Close on a stiff-bristled brush violently attacking pristine Brazilian walnut.

VALERIE VANCE is on her hands and knees. Her tailored blazer is crumpled in the corner. Her expensive silk blouse is soaked with sweat and speckled with bleach.

A galvanized bucket of suds sits beside her, surrounded by discarded, chemical-soaked rags.

She scrubs frantically at the floorboards. There is absolutely no stain. The wood is immaculate.

VALERIE
(muttering)
Routine maintenance. Just preventative upkeep. High-yield assets require... aggressive staging.

She scrubs harder. Her knuckles are raw and red.

VALERIE
We maximize the curb appeal. We protect the investment. We--

A low, bass-heavy VIBRATION rolls through the floorboards.

Thrum.

Valerie freezes. She stares at the bucket. The soapy water ripples in perfect, rhythmic concentric circles.

Thrum.

VALERIE
No. We agreed on a timeline. 

Thrum. Thrum.

The tempo increases. A hungry, impatient heartbeat echoing up from the joists.

The bucket rattles against the wood. Bleach sloshes over the rim, sizzling faintly against the varnish.

Valerie’s breath hitches. Her icy veneer shatters.

She drops the brush. Her eyes dart to the pristine cuff of her silk sleeve. She spots an invisible imperfection.

She scratches at the cuff. Hard. Digging her manicured nails into the expensive fabric.

VALERIE
(voice trembling)
I am the exclusive listing agent. I dictate the escrow schedule. Not you.

The floorboards GROAN--a deafening sound like splintering bone. The vibration travels straight up Valerie's calves.

She scrambles backward, her designer heels slipping in the puddle of bleach.

Abandoning the bucket, she lunges for the exit.

Her hand clamps around the heavy brass doorknob.

Before she can turn it, the massive mahogany door violently SWINGS shut on its own.

SLAM.

The sheer force of the impact rips the brass knob clean out of her grip.

Valerie stumbles back. She looks at her empty, stinging palm.

The doorknob clatters uselessly onto the floorboards at her feet.

Valerie slams both hands against the flat, knob-less wood. She digs her fingernails into the mahogany frame.

CLICK.

The heavy iron deadbolt slides into place.

The thrumming stops. The bedroom falls into a suffocating, dead silence.

scenes.narrative_action: The room's dimensions physically begin to shrink. The expensive floral wallpaper violently peels back in long, curling strips, revealing wet, dark, pulsing plaster underneath. Valerie is forced backward as the walls inch inward, knocking over the bleach bucket and sending chemical-soaked water pooling across the floor. She scrambles away from the encroaching walls, pounding her fists against the unyielding mahogany door and screaming for help as the room compresses around her.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: Escalates the supernatural threat by physically manifesting the house's absolute dominance over its 'owner.' Micro-Conflict (><): Valerie desperately fights for physical space vs. the house actively crushing her to force submission. Value Shift (+/-): From trapped but defiant (-) to physically overwhelmed and panicked (-).
scenes.draft_text:
INT. MANSION - MASTER BEDROOM - CONTINUOUS

Valerie stands in the center of the expansive primary suite. A heavy bucket of industrial bleach rests at her designer heels.

A low, guttural groan vibrates through the floorboards. 

The crown molding cracks. Plaster dust rains down on Valerie's tailored blazer.

She stares at the far wall. The distance between the bay window and the bed is visibly shorter. 

VALERIE
(muttering)
Minor foundational settling. Nothing a custom load-bearing beam can't mitigate.

The wall shudders in response. 

Thick strips of expensive, hand-painted floral wallpaper violently peel backward. They curl like dead skin.

Underneath, there is no drywall. Just wet, dark, violently pulsing plaster. It glistens like raw, breathing muscle.

Valerie retreats. Her heel catches the bleach bucket. 

It tips. Gallons of toxic, chemical-soaked water flood across the imported oak floor.

The pulsing wall hisses as the bleach touches the baseboards. The room violently contracts. 

The walls inch inward. The square footage actively evaporates.

A frantic silverfish darts out from the peeling wallpaper, splashing into the toxic puddle of bleach. It thrashes, drowning.

Valerie drops to her knees. She scoops the tiny, writhing insect out of the chemical burn with her bare fingers.

VALERIE
I've got you. It's strictly cosmetic. 

She gently deposits the bug into her breast pocket. 

The ceiling groans, dropping a full foot. 

Valerie scrambles backward on her hands and knees. The wet plaster walls bulge, closing the gap.

She hits the solid mahogany bedroom door. 

She grabs the heavy brass handle. Yanks. It refuses to turn.

The room compresses further. The custom bed frame buckles and snaps with a deafening crack.

Valerie presses her back against the unyielding mahogany. Her icy veneer shatters. 

Her hands shake violently. She obsessively scrubs at an invisible stain on her expensive silk cuff, her breathing ragged.

VALERIE
Toby! 

She hammers her fists against the thick wood. 

VALERIE
Toby, get the master keys! The primary suite is-- the dimensions are non-compliant! 

The walls close to within five feet. The pulsing plaster oozes a thick, warm humidity.

VALERIE
(screaming)
Toby! Get me out of here! Help! 

She kicks the door, her polished veneer entirely gone. 

The room shrinks again, trapping her in a claustrophobic, fleshy box.

SMASH CUT TO:

scenes.narrative_action: Trapped in a claustrophobic, shrinking square of space, Valerie drops to her knees in the spilled bleach. Looking up at the pulsing, fleshy ceiling, her icy professional mask shatters. She screams a desperate bargain: she promises to bring the entity a massive feast, more souls than it has ever consumed, if it just lets her live. The walls abruptly halt their crushing advance. A long, agonizing beat of silence follows. The deadbolt slowly slides open with a mocking, deliberate click.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: Forces Valerie to make the ultimate dark pact, sealing the fate of her colleagues to save her own life. Micro-Conflict (><): Valerie negotiates for her immediate survival vs. the house demanding a verbal commitment to mass murder. Value Shift (+/-): From impending death (-) to traumatized, compromised survival (+).
scenes.draft_text:
INT. MANSION - MASTER BEDROOM - CONTINUOUS

The square footage is vanishing.

Drywall buckles inward with a wet, sickening groan. Plaster flakes away, exposing deep, bruised crimson muscle beneath.

VALERIE VANCE is forced down. Her knees slam into the hardwood, splashing into a puddle of spilled bleach.

The chemical stench violently clashes with the smell of raw, hot copper.

The icy professional is gone. Valerie’s hands shake uncontrollably. 

She obsessively scrubs at the cuffs of her ruined silk blouse, grinding the bleach into the fabric until her knuckles turn white.

The ceiling descends. It pulses with a slow, heavy heartbeat that rattles the recessed lighting.

Valerie looks up at the fleshy canopy closing in on her skull.

VALERIE
Stop! Let's renegotiate!

The walls inch closer. Three feet of space left. Two feet.

VALERIE
(screaming)
You're thinking small! One client is a drop in the bucket! Look at the long-term yield!

She scrubs her cuffs harder. The wet silk tears.

VALERIE
I have a network! I can bring you volume! An exclusive, invite-only open house!

The ceiling drops another inch. The warm, damp surface grazes the top of her hair.

VALERIE
The entire firm! The board of directors! A massive, high-yield acquisition! An absolute feast!

Valerie presses her trembling hands against the floorboards.

VALERIE
More than you have ever consumed. Just... let me broker the deal. Let me walk out of here.

She squeezes her eyes shut. Bracing for the crush of bone.

Silence.

The rhythmic pulsing halts. The walls freeze in place.

An agonizing, heavy stillness suffocates the tight space. Only the sound of Valerie’s ragged, panicked breathing.

Seconds bleed away. 

At the heavy oak bedroom door, mere inches from her face--

CLICK.

The thick brass deadbolt slowly slides open. 

A deliberate, mocking invitation.

SMASH CUT TO:

scenes.narrative_action: Valerie stumbles out of the bedroom, collapsing onto the hallway runner. She gasps for air, staring back into the now perfectly normal, expansive master bedroom. The floral wallpaper is pristine; the bucket sits upright. Her illusion of control is entirely shattered. She scrambles to her feet, abandoning her cleaning supplies, and sprints down the corridor, fleeing the property as fast as her heels will allow.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: Transitions Valerie from a state of arrogant management to pure, traumatized flight. Micro-Conflict (><): Valerie wants to rationalize what just happened vs. the terrifying reality of the house's power. Value Shift (+/-): From compromised survival (+) to sheer, fleeing terror (-).
scenes.draft_text:
INT. MANSION - HALLWAY - CONTINUOUS

Valerie bursts through the bedroom doorway, carried by pure momentum. She hits the hardwood, sliding hard onto a vintage Persian runner.

She collapses. Her chest heaves. Manicured hands claw at the woven fabric.

She looks back.

The master bedroom doorway frames a picture of utter tranquility. The floral wallpaper is immaculate.

The plastic cleaning bucket sits perfectly upright in the center of the room. Not a single drop of blood in sight.

A beat of dead silence. The house is digesting.

Valerie’s breath hitches. Her icy veneer shatters. She looks down at her expensive silk cuffs.

She rubs the fabric frantically. Scrubbing at a stain that isn't there. Over and over, her thumb digging into the silk.

She stares at the bucket. Her supplies. Her mess.

VALERIE
(breathless)
Turnkey. Move-in ready.

She abandons the supplies. Pushes herself up. Her heels slip against the polished floor.

She turns her back on the bedroom.

Valerie sprints down the long corridor. The staccato CLACK-CLACK-CLACK of her stilettos echoes like gunfire against the vaulted ceilings.

She doesn't look back.

SMASH CUT TO:

scenes.scene_heading: INT. REAL ESTATE AGENCY - VALERIE'S DESK - DAY
scenes.narrative_action: The bustling, brightly lit downtown agency. Valerie hunches over her desk, her hair slightly disheveled, a stark contrast to her usual flawless presentation. She frantically clicks through multiple glowing tabs on her monitor, balancing bloated offshore accounts and hiding records of impossible diamond sales. She types with manic speed, trying to build her financial fortress fast enough to escape the nightmare she has created.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: Re-establishes the mundane, corporate world while highlighting Valerie's desperate reliance on wealth as her only shield. Micro-Conflict (><): Valerie races to hide her illicit wealth vs. the digital trail she is leaving behind. Value Shift (+/-): From fleeing terror (-) to a fragile, anxious focus (+).
scenes.draft_text:
INT. REAL ESTATE AGENCY - VALERIE'S DESK - DAY

A cacophony of ringing phones and aggressive sales pitches. The fluorescent lights are merciless. 

VALERIE VANCE (30s) hunches over her dual monitors. A stark contrast to her immaculate armor--a jagged strand of hair hangs over her eye. Her breathing is shallow.

On the screen: Glowing tabs. "CAYMAN HOLDINGS L.L.C." "Uncut Gem Liquidation." 

Her manicured fingers hammer the keyboard. A progress bar crawls: Encrypting Transfer... 62%

TOBY HIGGINS (20s) appears at her shoulder. He clutches a thick binder to his chest. 

TOBY
Ms. Vance? The Pendelton escrow--

VALERIE
(eyes glued to screen)
Synergize it with the Q3 projections. I'm busy.

Toby lingers. He stares at her desk. It's a chaotic mess of Post-its and half-empty espresso cups. 

His hand twitches. He reaches out and gently slides her stapler so it sits at a perfect ninety-degree angle to her mousepad. 

TOBY
I was doing the routine compliance sweep. And, um. There's a blind trust. 

Valerie freezes. The progress bar hits 74%. 

TOBY
It just routed two million dollars. But there's no property attached. Just a string of... well, it looks like a serial number for a diamond appraisal?

Valerie’s left hand drops to her lap. Her thumb digs into the edge of her silk cuff, frantically scrubbing at a stain that isn't there. 

VALERIE
It’s a proactive liquidity measure. 

TOBY
For who? The buyer line is blank. 

VALERIE
High-net-worth individuals require agile escrow frameworks, Toby. They don't want their capital sitting in stagnant domestic pools. 

TOBY
Right, but the SEC--

VALERIE
Look at the imported travertine tile in the lobby. 

Toby blinks, completely thrown. He looks over his shoulder. 

TOBY
What? 

VALERIE
The aesthetic value. The permanence. It projects an unbreakable foundation. That’s what I’m doing. Building a financial fortress for our clients. 

TOBY
(stuttering)
I-I-I just think if Richard audits the ledger--

VALERIE
Richard is a legacy artifact. He doesn't conceptualize modern asset pivots!

Her voice cracks. Too loud. A nearby REALTOR glances over. 

Valerie swallows hard. She lowers her voice, leaning in. 

VALERIE
Are you questioning my closing strategy? 

TOBY
N-no. I just... the routing numbers--

BING. 

A tiny green checkmark flashes on Valerie's screen. Transfer Complete. Balance: $0.00.

Valerie exhales. The tension drains from her shoulders. The icy mask slides perfectly back into place. 

She reaches out and deliberately taps the stapler, knocking it out of Toby's perfect alignment. 

VALERIE
File the anomaly under miscellaneous capital gains. And fix your tie, Toby. You look like a distressed asset.

Toby swallows hard, clutching his binder tighter. He backs away into the bustling bullpen.

Valerie stares at the empty digital vault. Safe. For now.

scenes.scene_heading: INT. REAL ESTATE AGENCY - VALERIE'S DESK - CONTINUOUS
scenes.narrative_action: Richard Sterling silently glides up behind Valerie's chair. His eyes lock onto the staggering financial figures reflecting in her glasses before she can minimize the window. He forcefully slaps a thick stack of printed, highlighted bank statements directly onto her keyboard, startling her. He places his hands on the desk on either side of her, physically boxing her into her cubicle.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: Introduces the human antagonist's counter-attack, forcing Valerie's supernatural secret into the corporate light. Micro-Conflict (><): Valerie tries to conceal her screen vs. Richard physically cornering her with hard evidence. Value Shift (+/-): From fragile focus (+) to suddenly exposed and trapped (-).
scenes.draft_text:
INT. REAL ESTATE AGENCY - VALERIE'S DESK - CONTINUOUS

The glow of a computer monitor illuminates VALERIE VANCE.

Reflected in the lenses of her designer glasses: a sprawling spreadsheet. Offshore account balances. Seven figures.

A shadow eclipses the glare.

RICHARD STERLING glides to a halt directly behind her chair. Silent. His eyes lock onto the staggering reflections in her lenses.

Valerie's hand twitches. She clicks the mouse. The spreadsheet vanishes.

THWACK.

A thick stack of paper slams down onto her keyboard. Bank statements. Saturated in neon-yellow highlighter.

Richard leans over her. He plants his palms flat on the desk, physically boxing her shoulders in.

RICHARD
You know what fascinates me about the deep ocean, Valerie? The pressure. It crushes steel submarines. But the bottom-feeders? They just adapt and gorge.

Valerie does not flinch. She stares straight ahead at the blank monitor.

VALERIE
If we are discussing the coastal portfolio, Richard, the structural integrity of those foundations can withstand significant tidal stress.

RICHARD
I am looking at the escrow accounts for the Blackwood mansion.

He drags a manicured fingernail across the top bank statement.

RICHARD
(cont'd)
These numbers are mutating. Five million in unverified deposits. No paper trail. No buyers. Just raw capital manifesting out of the drywall.

VALERIE
It is a highly liquid asset. We are seeing unprecedented appreciation. Unorthodox bidding wars.

Richard smiles. Calm. Utterly unnerving.

RICHARD
Unorthodox. You were underwater on your own mortgage last week. Now you are moving GDP-level cash.

VALERIE
My personal finances are a private renovation. The closing protocols on Blackwood are fully compliant.

Richard's smile vanishes. He closes the gap, his face inches from her ear. The sophisticated facade shatters.

RICHARD
(whispering through clenched teeth)
Do not insult my intelligence. You are a drowning woman who suddenly found a private island. I want the deed. I want the listing.

Valerie's breath hitches. Her thumb drops to her wrist, aggressively scrubbing at an invisible stain on her pristine white cuff.

VALERIE
The seller is extremely particular. They require a delicate touch. They do not tolerate aggressive representation.

RICHARD
Then I will eat the seller, too.

Richard pushes off the desk. He straightens his silk tie. The calm, predatory smile returns.

RICHARD
(cont'd)
Transfer the exclusive listing to my name by the gala. Or I will gut your broker's license and have you selling timeshares out of a strip mall.

He turns and strides across the bustling office.

Valerie is left boxed in by her own cubicle walls. She stares down at the neon-yellow highlighted numbers.

CUT TO:

scenes.scene_heading: INT. REAL ESTATE AGENCY - VALERIE'S DESK - CONTINUOUS
scenes.narrative_action: Richard taps the bank statements, demanding an immediate explanation for the massive influx of cash tied to the supposedly un-sellable modernist property. He orders Valerie to host a lavish, firm-wide open house gala to showcase the listing to their elite clientele. Valerie adamantly refuses. She desperately cites severe structural liabilities and the need for extreme discretion, using rapid-fire real estate buzzwords to sanitize her underlying panic.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: Defines the core conflict of the sequence: Richard's greed demanding a public spectacle versus Valerie's terror demanding containment. Micro-Conflict (><): Richard demands a massive public gala vs. Valerie fighting to keep the deadly property quarantined. Value Shift (+/-): From exposed (-) to aggressively defensive (+).
scenes.draft_text:
INT. REAL ESTATE AGENCY - VALERIE'S DESK - CONTINUOUS

A thick stack of bank statements slaps onto the glass. 

Richard’s manicured hand remains flat over the paper. He offers a calm, perfectly white smile.

RICHARD
An ecosystem thrives on shared resources, Valerie. So when a stagnant pond suddenly overflows with Cayman wire transfers...

VALERIE
It’s a bespoke escrow arrangement. 

Valerie’s eyes remain fixed on her monitor. 

VALERIE
High-net-worth liquidity requires tiered deposits. 

RICHARD
For the Blackwood property. That modernist tomb sat rotting on the market for three years. Now it’s generating the GDP of a small island.

VALERIE
I tapped a highly motivated demographic. They value a frictionless transaction.

RICHARD
Clearly. Which is why we’re throwing a gala.

Valerie’s fingers freeze over her keyboard.

VALERIE
No. 

RICHARD
Every whale in our Rolodex walking those hardwood floors by Friday night. Champagne, caviar, the works.

VALERIE
We can't. The property demands a rigorously curated acquisition strategy. A mass-market spectacle completely dilutes the exclusivity footprint.

RICHARD
I’m not asking for your marketing thesis.

VALERIE
Richard, the liability profile is a nightmare. We have unresolved load-bearing anomalies in the sub-basement. Unpermitted foundational settling. If a guest trips on a cantilevered threshold--

RICHARD
Then we buy a rug.

VALERIE
It's a strict NDA environment! The sellers demand absolute quarantine of the grounds. The structural integrity is severely compromised.

RICHARD
(smiling)
Are you trying to kill my appetite, Val? Because it just makes me hungrier.

VALERIE
I am protecting the firm from catastrophic litigation. 

Richard leans across the glass desk. He completely eclipses her monitor, invading her airspace. The smile vanishes. 

RICHARD
(whispering)
You work for me. This firm takes its cut of whatever golden goose you've locked inside that house. 

Valerie’s left hand drops to her lap. Her thumb violently scrubs the edge of her French silk cuff. Erasing an invisible stain. 

RICHARD
Friday night. Eight hundred invites. Or I pull your broker's license and hand the keys to Gemma. 

Richard straightens up. He casually adjusts his gold tie clip. 

RICHARD
It’s going to be a feeding frenzy. 

He turns and strides toward his corner office. 

Valerie stares down at the bank statements. She keeps scrubbing her cuff. The silk begins to fray.

CUT TO:

scenes.scene_heading: INT. REAL ESTATE AGENCY - VALERIE'S DESK - CONTINUOUS
scenes.narrative_action: Valerie's rejection instantly shatters Richard's polished, sophisticated facade. He violently kicks the base of Valerie's chair, spinning her around and physically trapping her legs beneath the heavy desk. He leans in dangerously close, his expensive cologne suffocating her. Invading her personal space, he drops his voice to a venomous whisper through clenched teeth, threatening to launch a full forensic audit and permanently destroy her career if she defies him.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: The climax of the sequence's micro-conflict; Richard asserts ultimate physical and professional dominance, utilizing his specific subtlety guideline under high stress. Micro-Conflict (><): Valerie tries to hold her boundary vs. Richard physically intimidating her into submission. Value Shift (+/-): From aggressively defensive (+) to physically and professionally threatened (-).
scenes.draft_text:
INT. REAL ESTATE AGENCY - VALERIE'S DESK - CONTINUOUS

The rejection hangs in the sterile office air.

Valerie keeps her chin leveled. 

Richard stares down at her. The sophisticated, unbothered smile slowly melts off his face, leaving something entirely hollow behind. 

He steps forward. 

WHAM. 

Richard kicks the aluminum base of Valerie's chair with the heel of his Oxford. 

The chair spins violently. Before Valerie can react, Richard slams both hands onto her armrests. 

He shoves the chair forward, pinning Valerie's legs painfully beneath the thick mahogany desk.

She tries to roll back. The wheels are locked against his shoes.

Richard leans in. Millimeters away. The suffocating, metallic stench of his expensive cologne floods the cramped space. 

RICHARD
(whispering through clenched teeth)
You look at a drought and think you've learned how to drink sand. 

Valerie's breathing turns shallow. She refuses to break eye contact.

VALERIE
The spatial dynamics of this office are highly visible, Richard. Step back.

He doesn't flinch. He just presses his weight heavier onto the armrests. 

RICHARD
Nature demands a hierarchy. You are attempting to disrupt the food chain over one modernist glass box. 

VALERIE
It is a turnkey architectural marvel. It requires an exclusive touch. 

RICHARD
It requires capital. Which you do not have. 

Valerie's left hand twitches. Her thumb drops to her wrist, obsessively scrubbing at an invisible blemish on her silk cuff. 

RICHARD
(still whispering)
Monday morning. I am launching a full forensic audit on your entire portfolio. 

Valerie goes completely still. The scrubbing stops. 

RICHARD
Every escrow account. Every shell LLC. Every mysterious buyer who toured that property and vanished without filing paperwork. 

He tilts his head, his nose almost brushing her cheek. 

RICHARD
I will turn your life into a microscopic slide. And when the board sees what a desperate, fraudulent little parasite you actually are... you won't even be qualified to lease a parking space.

Valerie's chest rises and falls rapidly. 

VALERIE
(voice trembling)
You destroy my escrow, you compromise the firm's quarterly valuation. 

RICHARD
I will burn the forest down just to watch you choke on the ash. 

He holds her gaze for three agonizing seconds. 

Then, Richard stands up straight. He smoothly adjusts his pristine silk tie. The predatory smile returns. 

RICHARD
Have a productive afternoon, Val.

He strides away toward his glass-walled office. 

Valerie remains pinned beneath the desk. Her hands shake violently. She goes back to scrubbing her cuff, digging her nails into the fabric until the threads begin to snap.

scenes.scene_heading: INT. REAL ESTATE AGENCY - VALERIE'S DESK - CONTINUOUS
scenes.narrative_action: Valerie stares into Richard's ruthless eyes, her breathing shallow. She looks from the damning bank statements to Richard's cruel smile, realizing she is pinned between a human predator and a supernatural beast. The crushing weight of her secret forces her to capitulate. She nods weakly, verbally agreeing to organize the gala and deliver the agency's elite. Richard smirks, steps back to adjust his suit, and walks away, entirely oblivious to the horrific death sentence he just signed for himself.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: The final value shift of the sequence; Valerie officially expands the house's menu to include her colleagues, fulfilling her dark pact. Micro-Conflict (><): Valerie's conscience vs. her overwhelming fear of poverty and ruin. Value Shift (+/-): From threatened (-) to defeated subservience (-).
scenes.draft_text:
INT. REAL ESTATE AGENCY - VALERIE'S DESK - CONTINUOUS

The red ink on the bank statements screams up from the mahogany desk. 

Valerie’s chest rises and falls in shallow, jagged increments. She stares across the paper at RICHARD.

RICHARD
(calm, unnerving smile)
An ecosystem only supports so many apex predators, Valerie. The rest is just... chum. Right now, your quarterly numbers are bleeding in the water.

Valerie avoids his gaze. Her thumb finds the edge of her immaculate silk cuff. She scrubs at a microscopic, nonexistent blemish.

VALERIE
The market is recalibrating. The architectural provenance of the property requires a highly curated buyer profile. It’s not a volume play. 

Richard leans over the desk. The scent of aggressive cologne and stale espresso invades her airspace. 

RICHARD
(whispering)
I don't care about the provenance. I care about the prestige. You are hoarding the keys to the most lucrative transaction in this firm's history, while quietly drowning in a kiddie pool of debt.

He taps the damning bank statements. 

RICHARD
I am absorbing the listing. Unless you prove you can feed the firm.

Valerie stops scrubbing her cuff. Her skin is red. 

She looks past Richard's shoulder. Out on the open floor plan, dozens of agents chatter. Gemma laughing by the corner office. Toby organizing a stack of glossy brochures. 

A buffet.

VALERIE
We leverage the exclusivity.

RICHARD
Go on.

VALERIE
A twilight gala. Invite-only. The platinum tier. I’ll bring the entire board, the top producers, the high-net-worth developers. We feed them the illusion of absolute scarcity.

Richard studies her face. The predatory smile widens. 

RICHARD
You host it. You deliver the elite. Or Monday morning, your desk is cleared.

Valerie swallows the bile rising in her throat. She looks at the human predator in front of her, then thinks of the concrete beast waiting in the hills. 

VALERIE
I'll make the arrangements. They'll all be there.

Richard smirks. He steps back, shooting his cuffs to adjust his tailored suit jacket. 

RICHARD
See? Adaptation. It's a beautiful thing to watch.

He turns and strolls away toward his glass office, completely oblivious to the slaughter he just RSVP'd to.

scenes.narrative_action: Following her forced agreement with Richard, Valerie stands at the end of the driveway, watching luxury catering vans choke the entrance of the glowing modernist mansion. She tightly grips her clipboard, her icy professional veneer firmly in place as she directs oblivious florists and waitstaff into the belly of the beast. The house's exterior lights flicker in a slow, predatory rhythm, anticipating the feast. Valerie takes a deep, shaky breath and marches inside, crossing the threshold into her own trap.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: Establish the ticking clock and the arrival of the victims. Micro-Conflict (><): Valerie wants to maintain absolute control over the event logistics vs. the house's looming, uncontrollable hunger. Value Shift (+/-): From resigned dread (-) to proactive, false confidence (+).
scenes.draft_text:
EXT. MODERNIST MANSION - DUSK

The modernist mansion sits against the dying sun like a jagged obsidian tooth. Its massive floor-to-ceiling windows glow with an unnatural, warm amber. 

A fleet of black luxury catering vans choke the sweeping driveway. 

VALERIE VANCE (30s) stands at the perimeter, a clipboard pressed to her chest like a shield. Her silk blouse is immaculate. Her face, an icy, impenetrable mask. 

Waitstaff in crisp white shirts haul crates of champagne and avant-garde floral arrangements toward the massive open threshold. The belly of the beast.

A young CATERING WAITER (19), swimming in an ill-fitting uniform, rushes past. His foot catches on the gravel. 

CRASH. A dozen crystal flutes shatter across the pavement. 

The Waiter drops to his knees, his hands trembling as he scrambles to gather the razor-sharp shards. 

CATERING WAITER
(panicking)
I'm sorry. Please, don't tell my manager, I'll cover the cost, I just-- I really need this shift.

Valerie stares down at the kid's scuffed, taped-together shoes. The ghost of her own childhood poverty violently rattles its cage. 

She steps forward. Her designer heel crushes a piece of broken crystal into dust. 

VALERIE
Leave it. 

The Waiter looks up, terrified. 

VALERIE
(transactional, sharp)
The breakage overhead is completely amortized. What I require right now is seamless logistical flow. 

CATERING WAITER
You're not going to charge me?

VALERIE
I am pivoting our focus to the architectural aesthetic. We need an unobstructed sightline from the entryway to the sunken lounge. 

She points her pen toward the glowing, cavernous maw of the front doors. 

VALERIE
Go inside. Take a fresh tray to the east wing. Keep the energy kinetic. 

The Waiter exhales a massive breath of relief. 

CATERING WAITER
Thank you. Seriously. 

He hurries up the driveway and crosses the threshold, vanishing into the warm amber light. Another innocent soul delivered to the slaughter. 

Flicker. 

The mansion's exterior uplighting dims, then surges. A slow, rhythmic pulse. Like a stomach expanding. A low, subsonic hum vibrates the gravel under Valerie's feet. 

The icy veneer cracks. 

Valerie's eyes drop to her pristine French cuffs. Her thumb aggressively, viciously scrubs at the stark white fabric, trying to erase an invisible stain. 

The house hums louder. Impatient. 

Valerie stops scrubbing. Her jaw sets into stone. She takes a single, deep, shaky breath. 

She marches up the driveway, crossing the threshold into her own trap. 

SMASH CUT TO:

scenes.narrative_action: The gala is in full swing. Dozens of innocent caterers weave through a crowd of elite realtors and executives. Valerie stands frozen near the staircase, watching Richard, Toby, and a miraculously recovered Gemma mingle. Beneath her designer heels, the hardwood baseboards vibrate with a low, hungry hum. Realizing the house is growing impatient, Valerie's eyes dart through the crowd, desperately scanning the room for a socially acceptable sacrifice to appease the entity before it takes matters into its own hands.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: Escalate the tension by placing all major characters in the danger zone. Micro-Conflict (><): Valerie wants to keep the peace and buy time vs. the house physically vibrating with impatience. Value Shift (+/-): From false confidence (+) to urgent desperation (-).
scenes.draft_text:
INT. MANSION - GRAND FOYER - NIGHT

A string quartet battles the roar of a hundred wealthy, overlapping voices. Waitstaff weave through a sea of bespoke tuxedos and sequined gowns, balancing silver trays of caviar.

Valerie stands frozen at the base of the floating glass staircase. Her posture is rigid. Her smile is a flawless, icy shield. 

Beneath her Louboutins, the Brazilian walnut floorboards TREMBLE. A low, subsonic hum vibrates up her calves.

A passing caterer’s champagne flute rattles violently on its tray. Valerie snaps her hand out, steadying the glass before it tips.

VALERIE
Seamless finishes require seamless service. Keep moving.

The caterer scurries off. Valerie presses her heel hard into the floor. The hum deepens. A hungry stomach growling through the foundation.

GEMMA glides into Valerie’s periphery. She looks radiant, though a flesh-colored bandage peeks out from beneath her diamond choker. Gemma casually checks her reflection in the dark screen of her phone.

GEMMA
I have to admit, Val. I didn't think you had the bandwidth to pull this off. The lighting in here is incredibly forgiving. It takes years off you.

VALERIE
Notice the recessed halogens, Gemma. It maximizes the square footage without drawing attention to the structural load.

GEMMA
(laughing)
Always selling. You'd think your life depended on it.

RICHARD materializes behind Gemma. He slips a heavy hand onto Valerie’s shoulder, leaning in close enough to brush her ear. 

RICHARD
A shark doesn't stop swimming, Gemma. If it stops, it drowns. Valerie understands the ecosystem. The big fish eat, or the ocean rots.

Richard smiles. His eyes are dead and unblinking.

RICHARD
Though I must say, Valerie. Your crowning jewel feels... restless tonight.

VALERIE
The foundation is settling. Standard for new construction of this magnitude.

TOBY pushes through the crowd. He holds a stack of cocktail napkins, perfectly squared off at the edges.

TOBY
Ms. Vance? I’m sorry to interrupt, Mr. Sterling, but several guests are complaining about a draft. And there’s a grinding noise coming from the primary suite vents. I should really go up and inspect the ductwork.

The floorboards beneath Valerie's feet LURCH. A sharp, localized earthquake.

Valerie’s flawless veneer cracks. Her thumb drops to her silk cuff, obsessively rubbing at an invisible stain.

VALERIE
No.

TOBY
It will only take a second, I have the schematics--

VALERIE
(sharp)
I said no, Toby. The upper levels are strictly off-limits to unverified buyers. Go to the kitchen. Inventory the remaining hors d'oeuvres. Do not leave the kitchen.

Toby blinks, taken aback. He nods, practically fleeing toward the swinging doors. 

Gemma picks aggressively at a cuticle on her thumb. A tiny drop of blood wells up.

GEMMA
Well. Someone's highly leveraged tonight.

Another violent VIBRATION. A massive crystal chandelier above them sways.

Richard doesn't flinch. He just watches Valerie, a smirk playing on his lips.

RICHARD
I’ll leave you to manage your assets, Valerie. Before the bottom falls out.

Richard guides Gemma away toward the wet bar.

Left alone, Valerie grips the glass banister. The hum is deafening in her ears now. The house is done waiting. It wants a meal.

Her eyes dart across the grand foyer. Past the innocent caterers. Past the string quartet. 

She scans the elite crowd. Hunting.

Her gaze lands on a loud, red-faced HEDGE FUND MANAGER by the coat check, currently screaming at a teenage attendant over a misplaced scarf. 

Valerie releases her cuff. Her icy smile returns. 

She steps off the staircase and glides toward him.

CUT TO:

scenes.scene_heading: INT. MANSION - LIVING ROOM TO HALLWAY - NIGHT
scenes.narrative_action: Valerie spots a notoriously corrupt Building Inspector gorging himself on caviar. She intercepts him, flashing a predatory, transactional smile. Using rapid-fire real estate buzzwords to sanitize her true intentions, she flatters his ego and hints at a highly lucrative, private bribe. She physically guides him by the elbow, steering him away from the safety of the crowded living room and toward the dimly lit, isolated corridors at the back of the house.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: Execute Valerie's plan to feed the house a targeted, minor sacrifice. Micro-Conflict (><): Valerie wants to isolate the Inspector quickly vs. the Inspector's slow, arrogant distraction. Value Shift (+/-): From urgent desperation (-) to calculated control (+).
scenes.draft_text:
INT. MANSION - LIVING ROOM TO HALLWAY - NIGHT

A chaotic symphony of clinking crystal and jazz. The gala is in full swing. 

Valerie stands at the edge of the room. Her icy veneer fractures for a fraction of a second. She violently scrubs at an invisible stain on her silk cuff. 

Across the room: INSPECTOR GARRISON (50s, bloated, sweating through a cheap suit) hovers over an ice sculpture, shoveling Beluga caviar onto a cracker with a serving spoon. 

Valerie stops rubbing her cuff. Her posture straightens. 

She approaches the buffet. Next to Garrison’s plate, a small honeybee struggles, its wings glued to the marble by a spilled puddle of champagne. 

Valerie slides a stiff cocktail napkin under the bee. She gently lifts it, placing it safely onto the dry leaves of a nearby Monstera plant. 

She turns to Garrison. A predatory, flawless smile locks into place.

VALERIE
Careful with the Beluga, Garrison. The sodium content is a liability.

Garrison swallows hard, wiping a stray fish egg from his chin.

GARRISON
I’m off the clock, Vance. 

VALERIE
Of course. But as a man who appreciates unparalleled premium assets, I thought you’d want first look at a high-yield zoning anomaly. 

Garrison reaches for another cracker. 

GARRISON
I signed off on the permits. We’re done. 

VALERIE
You signed off on the public floor plan. You didn't see the unlisted subterranean footprint. 

Garrison pauses. The cracker hovers near his mouth.

VALERIE
Pure, unregulated square footage. A structural oversight that requires a very specific, off-the-books appraisal. Highly lucrative for the right visionary.

Garrison lowers the cracker. Greed eclipses the gluttony. 

GARRISON
How far off the books?

Valerie smoothly hooks her arm through his, her manicured fingers pressing into his cheap polyester elbow. 

VALERIE
Let me walk you through the comps. 

She steers him away from the buffet. 

They weave through the oblivious, wealthy crowd, crossing the threshold from the blinding lights of the living room into the shadowed, narrow corridor of the west wing. 

The jazz music instantly muffles. The air grows unnaturally cold. 

Garrison slows his pace, glancing at the dark mahogany walls. 

GARRISON
Where are the fixtures? It’s a code violation, Vance. Pitch black back here.

VALERIE
(cheerfully)
Bespoke ambient integration. It requires a moment to adjust. Keep walking.

She tightens her grip on his arm, pulling him deeper into the dark. 

The floorboards beneath them let out a low, hungry groan. 

Valerie’s smile widens in the shadows. 

SMASH CUT TO:

scenes.narrative_action: Valerie ushers the Inspector into the windowless coatroom. The moment the heavy door clicks shut, the architecture reacts. The ceiling violently and silently compresses downward. Valerie steps back, watching with cold detachment as the corrupt official is crushed flat without a single drop of blood being spilled. A pristine emerald clatters onto the floorboards. Valerie snatches the gem, pockets it, and exhales, believing she has successfully satiated the beast.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: Deliver the false victory and demonstrate the house's bloodless consumption. Micro-Conflict (><): Valerie wants a quick, clean kill to buy time vs. the brutal, physical reality of the house's murder method. Value Shift (+/-): From calculated control (+) to profound relief (+).
scenes.draft_text:
INT. MANSION - COATROOM - NIGHT

INSPECTOR GARRITY (50s, cheap suit, heavy sweat) taps a metal pen against the windowless, mahogany-paneled walls. 

VALERIE VANCE stands in the doorway. Perfect posture. Icy, practiced smile.

GARRITY
No ventilation. No secondary egress. I’m looking at four code violations just standing here, Vance.

VALERIE
It’s a bespoke, climate-sealed garment gallery. The previous owners prioritized atmospheric preservation for vintage furs.

GARRITY
Call it whatever you want. It’s a fire hazard. Like the rest of this modernist death-trap.

VALERIE
The architecture is highly responsive to its occupants.

GARRITY
Yeah, well, my wallet isn't responding. We had an arrangement. Fifty grand, or I red-tag the foundation at dawn.

Valerie’s smile tightens. Her thumb aggressively rubs at an invisible stain on her pristine silk cuff.

VALERIE
The market is fluid right now. If you could just grant a temporary variance--

GARRITY
You’re drowning, Valerie. The bank is circling your firm, and you don’t have a dime. Stop pitching me and pay up.

Valerie stops scrubbing her cuff. The desperation vanishes, replaced by a chilling calm. 

She steps backward, her heels crossing the threshold into the main hall.

VALERIE
You’re right. Let’s focus on the immediate appraisal. Look closely at the crown molding. It’s a truly immersive design.

Valerie pulls the heavy brass handle.

GARRITY
Hey--

The thick oak door swings shut. 

CLICK.

Garrity lunges for the knob. It refuses to turn. He bangs a heavy fist against the mahogany.

GARRITY
Vance! Open this door!

Above him, the shadows shift. 

Garrity looks up.

The ten-foot ceiling is no longer at ten feet. It’s at eight. 

Now six.

It descends violently, yet completely silently. A pneumatic press of plaster and steel. 

Garrity opens his mouth to scream.

AT THE DOOR

Valerie leans against the heavy oak. She presses her eye to the wide, vintage brass keyhole.

Watching. Cold. Detached.

THROUGH THE KEYHOLE

The ceiling meets Garrity’s shoulders.

He buckles. 

No blood sprays. No gore splatters the pristine mahogany. Instead, a sickening, dry CRUNCH echoes through the wood. 

The house simply folds him inward, absorbing his mass like dry earth drinking a drop of water.

IN THE HALLWAY

Valerie stands upright. She waits three seconds. 

She turns the knob.

INT. MANSION - COATROOM - CONTINUOUS

The door swings open. 

The ceiling rests peacefully at ten feet. 

The coatroom is entirely empty.

Valerie steps inside. Her eyes scan the flawless floorboards.

There. Resting in the center of the room. 

A pristine, golf-ball-sized EMERALD.

Valerie drops to her knees. She snatches the gem. Its sharp edges dig into her palm. 

She pockets the stone, pressing her hand flat against her thigh to feel its hard, heavy salvation.

A long, shuddering exhale escapes her lips.

CUT TO:

scenes.narrative_action: Valerie returns to the gala, but the relief is instantly shattered. The mansion's low, rhythmic hum intensifies into a bone-rattling vibration, completely rejecting her token offering. Right before Valerie's eyes, the architectural geography begins to shift. The main hallway visibly stretches, the walls elongating and pulling the heavy front doors further away from the oblivious guests in the living room. Valerie physically shoves past a waiter, realizing the entity is actively sealing off all escape routes.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: Reveal the monster's true power and initiate the sequence climax. Micro-Conflict (><): Valerie wants to believe her sacrifice worked vs. the house independently altering its physical geometry. Value Shift (+/-): From profound relief (+) to mounting horror (-).
scenes.draft_text:
INT. MANSION - GRAND FOYER - NIGHT

Valerie steps out of the shadows and into the soft, warm light of the foyer. 

From the adjacent living room, the clinking of crystal and a lively jazz quartet bleed through the archway.

She exhales. A long, shuddering release of breath.

She smooths the lapels of her designer blazer. Safe. The transaction is complete.

But the floorboards disagree.

A low, rhythmic hum vibrates through the soles of her stilettos.

Valerie freezes. 

The hum deepens. It climbs her legs. A bone-rattling frequency that makes her teeth ache.

She looks back toward the living room. Dozens of WEALTHY GUESTS laugh and drink, completely oblivious to the tremor.

Valerie turns her attention to the massive, double oak front doors. Thirty feet away.

Groaaan.

The sound doesn't come from the wood. It comes from the drywall.

Valerie watches, paralyzed, as the architectural geometry of the hallway begins to mutate.

The pristine crown molding stretches like warm taffy.

The front doors slide backward. Forty feet away.

The hardwood floor extends. Planks duplicate with a sickening, wet crunch perfectly masked by the jazz bassline.

A WAITER steps directly into her line of sight, balancing a silver tray of champagne flutes.

WAITER
Fresh glass, Miss Vance?

VALERIE
(eyes locked on the doors)
Clear the tray. We're closing the showing.

WAITER
But Mr. Sterling requested a toast in five minutes. He asked me to--

VALERIE
I am the listing agent. I dictate the timeline. Move.

The floor vibrates violently. The champagne ripples in the crystal. 

The Waiter just smiles, oblivious to the shifting physics of the room.

WAITER
It really is a beautiful property. So spacious.

Fifty feet. Sixty feet. The hallway elongates into a cavernous, impossible tunnel.

The house rejected the token offering. It wants the whole buffet.

Valerie shoves the Waiter. 

Hard.

The silver tray flips. Crystal shatters across the stretching floorboards.

Valerie sprints down the impossible corridor.

The walls groan, accelerating their stretch, pulling the exit further into the dark.

SLAM.

The heavy oak doors crash shut at the far end of the tunnel.

Four heavy deadbolts slide into place with a wet, meaty thwack.

Valerie stops dead in the center of the warped hallway.

Chest heaving. Trapped.

Her trembling fingers drop to her wrists. 

She begins to scrub at the edge of her expensive silk cuffs. 

Scrubbing violently at a stain that isn't there.

SMASH CUT TO:

scenes.narrative_action: Valerie moves frantically through the crowd, trying to subtly herd the wealthy guests toward the retreating exits without causing a stampede. Gemma steps directly into Valerie's path, blocking her. Feeling the unnatural vibrations but misinterpreting them as social tension, Gemma aggressively picks at her own cuticles until they bleed, accusing Valerie of trying to ice her out of networking opportunities. Valerie is physically trapped by Gemma's petty, passive-aggressive confrontation while the room stretches around them.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: Delay the protagonist from saving the innocent, compounding her guilt. Micro-Conflict (><): Valerie needs to evacuate the room immediately vs. Gemma's relentless, petty jealousy anchoring them in place. Value Shift (+/-): From mounting horror (-) to trapped panic (-).
scenes.draft_text:
INT. MANSION - LIVING ROOM - NIGHT

A sea of tuxedoed politicians and sequined socialites clinks glasses. 

The floorboards hum. A low, subsonic vibration. 

VALERIE weaves through the dense crowd. Her eyes dart to the vaulted ceiling. The mahogany beams groan, subtly bowing inward. 

She herds a cluster of oblivious hedge-fund managers toward the massive glass exit doors.

VALERIE
If we could just migrate toward the foyer, gentlemen. The cross-ventilation is much more robust by the entryway. 

She smiles, projecting a flawless, icy mask. Beneath it, her thumb ruthlessly scrubs at an invisible stain on her silk cuff. 

She turns back to grab the stragglers. 

GEMMA steps dead into her path. 

Gemma’s smile is razor-sharp. She holds a champagne flute in one hand, her phone in the other. 

GEMMA
Val, sweetie. You’re practically shoving the Mayor’s chief of staff out the door. 

VALERIE
Just optimizing the traffic flow, Gemma. The HVAC is acting up. We need everyone on the terrace.

Valerie tries to sidestep. Gemma mirrors her, blocking the route. 

GEMMA
(fake pout)
And miss the ambiance? It’s a triumph, really. Though the lighting in here is doing absolutely nothing for your complexion. 

The floor vibrates again. Harder this time. 

Gemma’s smile falters for a fraction of a second. She feels the unnatural shift in the room. Her eyes narrow at Valerie. 

GEMMA
Is that why you’re rushing everyone out? Because I finally got five minutes with the Sterling reps? 

Gemma’s thumb finds her index finger. She digs her nail into her own cuticle. 

VALERIE
This isn't about the Sterling account. Move. 

GEMMA
You always were so territorial. 

Gemma picks harder. The skin tears. A bead of dark crimson wells up on her cuticle. 

The living room stretches. The distance between the fireplace and the exit elongates by ten impossible feet. The shadows in the corners deepen, pooling like thick oil.

Valerie grabs Gemma’s arm. 

VALERIE
Gemma, look at the walls. The foundational load is failing. We are leaving.

Gemma yanks her arm away. She raises her phone, casually checking her reflection in the dark screen. She smears the drop of blood from her cuticle across the glass.

GEMMA
The only thing failing here is your monopoly on this zip code. You really thought you could ice me out of a commission this size? 

A crystal chandelier sways violently overhead. The glass teardrops chime against each other. A sickening, hungry sound. 

Valerie stares at the exit, now impossibly far away. Trapped behind Gemma’s immovable, bleeding ego. 

SMASH CUT TO:

scenes.narrative_action: With a deafening, synchronized crash, thick, impenetrable steel shutters slam down over every window and exterior door. The string quartet abruptly stops playing. The ambient lighting shifts to a bruised, sickly purple, plunging the gala into a claustrophobic twilight. Wealthy partygoers murmur in confusion, champagne flutes trembling. Richard storms over to Valerie, his sophisticated facade breaking. He physically invades her personal space, whispering violent threats through clenched teeth, demanding to know what kind of PR stunt she is pulling.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: Spring the trap completely, removing all hope of easy escape. Micro-Conflict (><): Richard demands answers and attempts to assert dominance vs. Valerie's total loss of control over the supernatural environment. Value Shift (+/-): From trapped panic (-) to absolute, suffocating terror (-).
scenes.draft_text:
INT. MANSION - LIVING ROOM - NIGHT

CRASH. 

Thick, impenetrable steel shutters guillotine down over the floor-to-ceiling windows. 

BAM. BAM. BAM. The exterior doors seal shut.

The string quartet's Vivaldi screeches to a halt. A frantic bow snaps a cello string. 

The warm, ambient gala lighting stutters. It bleeds into a bruised, sickly purple. A claustrophobic twilight coats the room. 

Dozens of wealthy partygoers freeze. A collective, panicked murmur ripples through the crowd. Champagne trembles in crystal flutes. 

VALERIE stands paralyzed near the grand staircase. 

RICHARD shoves past a bewildered tech executive. His sophisticated facade is completely gone. 

He storms toward Valerie, backing her against the cold marble banister. He invades her personal space, nose-to-nose. 

RICHARD
(whispering through clenched teeth)
End this avant-garde immersive bullshit right now.

VALERIE
It's a... a high-end security feature. Automated perimeter sealing. 

Valerie's hands shake. She obsessively scrubs her thumb against the edge of her silk cuff, trying to erase a stain that isn't there.

RICHARD
You don't lock my clients in a box. You don't cage the apex predators. 

VALERIE
Richard, please, step back. The smart-home mainframe just needs a hard reboot--

RICHARD
I will dismantle your entire life. I will strip your license so fast you'll be leasing squatter dens by morning. Open the goddamn doors. 

A deep, guttural vibration rumbles through the hardwood floorboards. The crystal chandelier rattles above them. 

The sickly purple light pulses. Squeezing inward. Like a stomach contracting.

VALERIE
I didn't trigger the lockdown. 

RICHARD
Fix it. Before I ruin you.

Valerie stares into the pulsing purple shadows of the vaulted ceiling. 

VALERIE
I can't. The property is... renegotiating. 

A terrified SCREAM echoes from the kitchen wing. 

SMASH CUT TO:

scenes.narrative_action: Valerie screams for everyone to get back, but the groaning of the floorboards drowns her out. The polished hardwood violently splits open, transforming into a series of jagged, wooden maws spanning the entire room. Screaming waitstaff and terrified executives lose their footing and plunge into the dark, expanding voids beneath the house. The air fills with the coppery whiff of death as the explosive, chaotic violence consumes the innocent and the corrupt alike. Valerie falls to her knees, screaming for the house to stop, but it completely ignores her pleas.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: Deliver the 'All Is Lost' massacre and the ultimate consequence of Valerie's greed. Micro-Conflict (><): Valerie desperately tries to halt the slaughter vs. the house unleashing its full, unstoppable feeding frenzy. Value Shift (+/-): From suffocating terror (-) to absolute, soul-crushing despair (-).
scenes.draft_text:
INT. MANSION - LIVING ROOM - CONTINUOUS

The gala is paralyzed. A hundred wealthy guests stare at the vibrating floor. 

Valerie shoves through a wall of bespoke tuxedos. 

VALERIE
Get away from the center! Everyone, out the front! Now!

A tectonic GROAN reverberates through the foundation. It rattles the fillings in Valerie’s teeth. 

Her voice is entirely drowned out. 

Champagne flutes vibrate off silver trays. Glass shatters across the Brazilian walnut. 

VALERIE
(screaming)
The doors! Move!
