The center of the room bows upward. 

With the deafening crack of artillery fire, the polished hardwood violently splits open.

A massive fault line tears from the fireplace to the foyer. 

The floorboards don't just break. They bend. They snap into jagged, asymmetrical spikes. 

A series of massive, wooden maws spans the entire living room. 

Total pandemonium. 

Wealthy EXECUTIVES trample each other. They claw at expensive gowns, pulling women down to vault over them. 

A terrified WAITRESS backpedals. Her heel catches on the splintered wood. 

She slides backward toward the expanding, pitch-black void. 

Valerie lunges across the buckling floor. She dives, grabbing the Waitress by the wrist. 

WAITRESS
Pull me up! Please! 

VALERIE
I’ve got you! Just hold--

The house shudders. The wooden maw snaps wider. 

The Waitress is jerked downward. Her fingers slip from Valerie’s grip. 

She vanishes into the dark. 

A wet, heavy CRUNCH echoes from the basement. 

Valerie recoils, scrambling backward on her hands and knees. 

A fine, crimson mist sprays up from the chasm. The heavy, coppery stench of fresh blood coats the back of Valerie's throat. 

The house is feeding. 

To her left, a TECH EXECUTIVE tries to climb a mid-century bookshelf. 

The wall literally leans inward. The bookshelf folds over, dumping him directly into a fresh, gaping hole in the floor. 

He screams until a wooden beam snaps shut over the opening like a jaw. 

VALERIE
No! Stop! 

Valerie crawls to the edge of the central chasm. 

VALERIE
(to the house)
We had an arrangement! This isn't the deal!

The house answers with another deep, rumbling swallow. 

More floorboards erupt. The perimeter of the room shrinks. 

A corrupt CITY COUNCILMAN shoves a young BARTENDER into the pit to save his own balance. 

The house doesn't care. A secondary maw opens beneath the Councilman's feet. 

He plummets into the dark. 

Explosive, chaotic violence consumes the room. The innocent and the corrupt fall together. 

Blood spatters across Valerie’s pristine white blazer. 

She pushes herself up, staggering on a shrinking island of solid floor. 

VALERIE
I said stop! You’re voiding the contract! 

The walls groan, ignoring her completely. 

The screams begin to thin out. They are replaced by the sickening sound of grinding timber and wet tearing. 

Valerie stares at the carnage. The absolute, unstoppable scale of it. 

Her knees give out. She collapses onto the remaining hardwood. 

Her icy veneer shatters. She is hyperventilating. 

Valerie looks down at the fresh blood soaking into her expensive silk cuff. 

Her trembling fingers pinch the fabric. 

She starts scrubbing at the stain. 

VALERIE
(whispering)
It’s fine. It comes out. We can fix the terms. 

She scrubs harder. Frantic. Obsessive. 

VALERIE
We just need a new appraisal. 

The house lets out a long, satisfied creak. 

Valerie scrubs at her cuff until her knuckles bleed, completely alone in the center of the slaughter.

SMASH CUT TO:

scenes.narrative_action: Amidst the chaotic slaughter, Toby is backed into a corner, paralyzed. He watches a young caterer claw at the edge of a wooden maw before being dragged screaming into the darkness. The incomprehensible horror shatters Toby's mind completely. He collapses to the floor, developing a severe, uncontrollable stutter. Oblivious to the screams around him, he compulsively begins organizing scattered, blood-spattered hors d'oeuvres trays into perfect right angles, mumbling nonsensically as his innocent psyche is destroyed.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: Dramatize the destruction of innocence, paying off Toby's arc tragically. Micro-Conflict (><): Toby's relentless, optimistic worldview vs. the undeniable, brutal reality of the supernatural slaughter. Value Shift (+/-): From paralyzed fear (-) to complete psychological fragmentation (-).
scenes.draft_text:
INT. MANSION - DINING AREA - CONTINUOUS

Screams drown out the string quartet. The gala is a slaughterhouse.

TOBY (20s) presses his back against the Venetian plaster. His eyes are wide, unblinking. 

Across the room, the mahogany floorboards violently splinter upward. They bend back, forming a jagged, wooden maw.

A YOUNG CATERER clings to the edge of the abyss. His fingernails tear against the polished wood. 

CATERER
Help! Somebody grab my hand!

Toby’s muscles twitch. He wants to move. He needs to help. 

He takes a half-step forward.

The floorboards snap like a bear trap. The wood crushes the caterer’s ribs. 

The boy is yanked down into the darkness. A wet crunch echoes over the screams. 

The floor seals shut. Perfectly flush. A single pool of crimson seeps through the tight seams.

Toby stops breathing. The relentless optimism drains from his face, replaced by a hollow, vacant stare. 

His knees buckle. He hits the floor hard.

Silver serving platters and shattered champagne flutes litter the rug. Blood splatters across a tray of ruined caviar blinis.

Toby stares at the chaotic mess. His hands begin to tremble. 

He reaches out. His bloody fingers grip a silver tray. He drags it across the rug, aligning it precisely parallel to the baseboard.

TOBY
(mumbling)
S-s-structural anomaly. T-the joists. The load-bearing...

He grabs a second tray. Slides it perfectly perpendicular to the first. A flawless ninety-degree angle.

TOBY
If the c-c-cantilever holds the weight... then the foundation...

A terrified GUEST sprints past, kicking a tray out of alignment. 

Toby doesn't look up at the screaming guest. He crawls forward on his hands and knees. 

He gently pulls the kicked tray back into its exact geometric position. 

TOBY
T-the foundation is perfectly sound. Just... just standard settling. 

He lines up a row of crushed champagne flutes. Spacing them exactly one inch apart. 

The mansion groans around him, walls contracting. 

Toby just keeps building his perfect, bloody grid.

SMASH CUT TO:

scenes.scene_heading: INT. MANSION - LIVING ROOM - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.narrative_action: The deafening screams slowly fade into a ringing, traumatized silence as the jagged floorboards seamlessly stitch themselves back together, leaving the room pristine and empty of victims. Valerie stands completely untouched in the center of the carnage, spared by the house so she can witness her sins. She looks at the empty, bloodless room, then down at Toby's broken, mumbling form in the corner. Under the supreme stress of realizing she has orchestrated a mass execution, Valerie's icy mask finally slips, and she obsessively scrubs at invisible stains on her expensive cuffs.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: Establish the devastating aftermath and Valerie's realization that she is the true monster. Micro-Conflict (><): Valerie's desire to view herself as a savvy businesswoman vs. the undeniable, physical proof of her monstrous actions. Value Shift (+/-): From soul-crushing despair (-) to hollow, traumatized guilt (-).
scenes.draft_text:
INT. MANSION - LIVING ROOM - MOMENTS LATER

The final, deafening scream cuts off. 

Replaced by a hollow, high-pitched ringing.

The wide-plank oak floorboards writhe. Jagged, splintered wood weaves together like knitting bone. 

Snap. Snap. Snap.

Seamless. Pristine. Not a single drop of blood remains.

VALERIE stands dead center in the architectural marvel. Her tailored blazer is immaculate. The house spared her. Kept her on the stage to view the empty theater.

In the far corner, a shadow shifts.

TOBY sits collapsed against the Venetian plaster. His knees pulled to his chest. 

His trembling fingers slide a pair of scattered screws and a broken brass hinge across the floor. He lines them up. Pushing them into a perfect, ninety-degree angle against the baseboard.

TOBY
(stuttering)
Th-they were... Mr. Sterling was right th-there. The floor just...

VALERIE
It's a foundational shift.

Valerie’s voice is robotic. A recording playing to an empty room.

VALERIE
Rapid settling. The sub-floor tension... it creates a vacuum effect. An architectural anomaly.

Toby nudges the screws. Perfectly parallel.

VALERIE
We just need to... we draft a non-disclosure amendment. Reassess the market value based on the structural--

TOBY
It ate them, Val.

Toby’s hands freeze. He looks up. His eyes are dead, entirely stripped of their usual earnest light. 

TOBY
It swallowed them whole. And it left you.

Valerie stares at the empty space where her boss and clients stood three minutes ago.

She opens her mouth. The spin dies in her throat. 

The silence of the house presses in on her. Heavy. Expectant. It feels like a satisfied exhale.

Valerie looks at her hands. 

Her right thumb moves to her left cuff. She rubs the silk. 

Once. Twice. 

She stares at the pristine white fabric. 

She scrubs harder. Digging her manicured nails into the weave. 

Her breathing fractures into rapid, shallow gasps. She scrubs the invisible stain, tearing at the expensive thread, unable to wash it away.

scenes.narrative_action: Valerie's hands drop from her cuffs as the ringing silence is broken by a low, wet rumble. The pristine floorboards beneath Toby suddenly soften, vibrating like a hungry stomach. Valerie snaps out of her shock, grabs Toby by his cheap suit collar, and hoists his catatonic body up just as the wood snaps shut like jaws. She physically drags his dead weight toward the foyer.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: Escaping the immediate aftermath of the massacre. Micro-Conflict: Valerie wants to save Toby vs. the house's immediate hunger. Value Shift: (+/-) From paralyzed shock (-) to mobilized survival (+).
scenes.draft_text:
INT. MANSION - LIVING ROOM - CONTINUOUS

Valerie’s manic fingers freeze. She drops her hands from her raw, red cuffs.

The ringing silence of the massacre hangs in the air. 

Then-- a low, wet RUMBLE vibrates through the Brazilian walnut floor. 

Toby kneels in the center of the room. Catatonic. 

Blood pools inches from his knees. He ignores it. His trembling fingers obsessively nudge a stray brass coaster, trying to align it perfectly parallel to the rug's geometric pattern.

TOBY
(whispering)
T-t-t-the structural integrity. It's... it's just settling. Minor subsidence. 

The floorboards beneath him ripple. The wood softens, losing its rigid grain, turning into a fleshy, gelatinous membrane. 

A deep, guttural churning echoes from the subfloor. The sound of an empty, starving stomach.

VALERIE
Toby. Vacate the footprint. Now.

Toby doesn't move. He nudges the coaster a fraction of an inch.

TOBY
I-I-I didn't f-file the hazard disclosures... 

The floorboards bow inward. A sinkhole of splintering wood and viscous, clear saliva opens beneath his dress shoes.

Valerie’s icy veneer shatters. She lunges.

Her manicured hands fist into the cheap polyester collar of Toby's suit. She plants her designer heels and heaves backward with everything she has.

SNAP.

The walnut planks slam shut like jagged teeth. 

They bite through empty air, pulverizing the brass coaster exactly where Toby’s torso was a millisecond ago.

Wood splinters rain down across the pristine rug.

Valerie lands hard on her back. Toby sprawls over her, a dead weight.

The room tilts. The drywall groans, expanding and contracting like a massive set of lungs. 

Valerie scrambles up. She hooks her arms under Toby's armpits. 

She physically drags his limp body backward toward the grand archway.

VALERIE
(panting)
We are relocating. High-traffic areas only.

Toby’s heels drag uselessly across the floor. 

TOBY
The c-c-commission-- Mr. Sterling said--

VALERIE
Screw Sterling! The foundation is compromised! 

The ceiling drops an inch. A wet, sucking sound echoes from the HVAC vents.

Valerie hauls him into the foyer, her breath ragged, her eyes locked on the shifting architecture.

CUT TO:

scenes.narrative_action: Valerie hauls Toby up the modernist floating staircase. The house fights back—the wooden treads tilt and slick over, trying to slide them back down into the living room. Valerie anchors herself on the glass railing, her designer heels violently digging into the gaps between the steps, and desperately yanks Toby over the lip of the second-floor landing.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: Establishing the house as an active, physical obstacle. Micro-Conflict: Valerie wants to reach the second floor vs. the sentient staircase trying to drop them. Value Shift: (+/-) From vulnerable in the open (-) to reaching temporary high ground (+).
scenes.draft_text:
INT. MANSION - GRAND STAIRCASE - CONTINUOUS

Valerie hauls Toby by his collar onto the bottom tread of the floating staircase. 

VALERIE
Move! The master suite offers unparalleled structural privacy--

TOBY
(stuttering)
V-Valerie, the cantilever brackets--

A deep, guttural GROAN echoes from the walls. 

The rich mahogany treads SHUDDER. 

TOBY
--they're not designed for dynamic torque!

The floating steps TILT forward. A sudden thirty-degree downward pitch. 

A viscous, high-gloss resin sweats instantly from the wood grain. The stairs become a frictionless slide. 

Toby’s loafers lose purchase. He slams hard onto his chest. 

He slides backward, accelerating toward the gaping maw of the living room below. 

VALERIE
Toby!

Toby scrambles, fingernails scraping uselessly against the slick premium finish. 

TOBY
The shear stress! It defies basic physics, it’s--

VALERIE
Grab my hand!

Valerie throws her body weight against the tempered glass railing. 

She kicks her right foot out. Wedges her designer stiletto violently into the narrow gap between two floating steps. 

The heel snaps into the joint. A makeshift anchor. 

She lunges down, catching Toby’s wrist just as his legs dangle over the bottom edge. 

The house RUMBLES. The staircase pitches steeper. Forty-five degrees. 

The glass railing bows outward under their combined weight. Spiderweb fractures splinter across the pane. 

TOBY
The glass! The tensile limit is failing! Let me go, you'll--

VALERIE
This is architectural-grade glazing! It adds immense resale value! 

She strains. Her tailored sleeve rides up, exposing her cuff. 

A speck of dirt smears the pristine white fabric. 

Despite the life-or-death physics, Valerie’s free hand twitches. She obsessively scrubs at the stain for one erratic, terrifying second. 

Then she snaps back. Grabs his forearm with both hands.

VALERIE
We are closing this goddamn listing!

She HEAVES. 

Her stiletto grinds against the mahogany. 

Toby flies upward. 

Valerie yanks him over the top lip of the staircase. 

They collapse hard onto the flat, carpeted expanse of the second-floor landing. 

Below them, the staircase violently snaps back into a perfect, level horizontal plane. Waiting.

scenes.narrative_action: Valerie drags Toby across the threshold and slams the heavy, reinforced steel-core door shut, throwing the deadbolt. The muffled, agonizing screams of the remaining gala guests suddenly vibrate through the plaster walls. Valerie collapses against the door, sliding down to the tile floor. Her icy professional mask completely shatters as she hyperventilates, covering her ears in absolute defeat.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: Isolating the characters for the Dark Night of the Soul. Micro-Conflict: Valerie wants to block out the horror vs. the auditory reality of the massacre penetrating the walls. Value Shift: (+/-) From adrenaline-fueled escape (+) to absolute, crushing despair (-).
scenes.draft_text:
INT. MANSION - MASTER BATHROOM - CONTINUOUS

Valerie hauls Toby over the marble threshold by his lapels. 

She throws her entire weight against the reinforced steel-core door. It SLAMS shut. She twists the heavy brass deadbolt. 

A heavy, sickening THUD strikes the other side of the wood. 

Then, the SCREAMS. Muffled, agonizing shrieks of the gala guests vibrate through the Venetian plaster walls. 

Toby collapses near the base of the dual vanity. He is bone-pale. His trembling hands reach up to a silver tray of luxury toiletries. 

He begins moving the glass bottles. Aligning them. Perfect ninety-degree angles.

TOBY
(stuttering)
T-the floor joists. They aren't settling. It's a... it's a peristaltic contraction. The load-bearing walls, Valerie. They're flexing. 

Valerie slides her back down the steel door until she hits the heated tile. Her icy veneer is gone. Her breath comes in jagged, shallow gasps. 

She stares at her pristine white cuff. She begins to scrub at it. There is no blood, but she scratches at the silk with her manicured nails. Frantic. 

VALERIE
The listing explicitly stated acoustic insulation. R-30 rating minimum. You shouldn't be able to hear... the ambient noise from the foyer.

A wet, tearing CRUNCH echoes through the plumbing. 

Toby flinches. He pushes a pumice stone exactly parallel to a bottle of eucalyptus wash. 

TOBY
I c-can't breathe. We're going to die in a half-bath.

VALERIE
Master en suite. It's a master en suite, Toby. Don't undersell the...

Another piercing SHRIEK vibrates the floorboards, cutting her off. The sound of a human body being dragged across hardwood. 

Valerie’s chest heaves. The hyperventilation takes over. She scrubs her cuff so hard the silk tears. 

TOBY
(softly)
I just wanted to sell a starter home. 

The walls throb. A deep, resonant hum of digestion. 

Valerie stops scrubbing. The real estate buzzwords fail her. She looks at the torn fabric of her sleeve, then up at the vibrating plaster. 

She presses both palms brutally hard over her ears. She pulls her knees to her chest, curling into a tight, trembling ball on the floor. 

Absolute defeat.

scenes.narrative_action: Valerie crawls across the floor toward Toby. She reaches into her tailored blazer, pulls out a handful of pristine, blood-free diamonds, and drops them onto the cold tiles between them. Weeping openly, she physically confesses the dark pact, pointing to the glittering gems as the literal price of the souls dying outside. She admits her hollow obsession with wealth blinded her to the monster she was feeding.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: The confession of the core sin. Micro-Conflict: Valerie wants absolution for her greed vs. Toby's growing, silent horror. Value Shift: (+/-) From secretive and burdened (-) to exposed and vulnerable (+).
scenes.draft_text:
INT. MANSION - MASTER BATHROOM - LATER

Muffled SCREAMS echo through the frosted glass door. A heavy thud. Then, the rhythmic grinding of architecture digesting bone.

The master bathroom is a cold tomb of imported Carrara marble. 

VALERIE drags herself across the heated tiles. Her tailored blazer is torn, her knees scraping the grout. 

TOBY sits huddled in the massive walk-in shower stall. Knees pulled to his chest. He stares blankly at the brass drain. 

Valerie reaches into her ruined blazer. Her hand trembles violently. 

She pulls out a heavy, clinking fistful of stones. Pristine, blood-free diamonds. 

She drops them. They scatter across the tiles between them with a sharp, terrifying clatter.

VALERIE
(breathless)
The ROI, Toby. Look at the return on investment.

Toby flinches at the sound. His eyes dart from the drain to the glittering pile.

VALERIE
Flawless clarity. Zero inclusions. The appraisal value is... it’s unprecedented.

TOBY
Are those...

Toby presses his back harder against the shower glass.

TOBY
M-Ms. Vance, what is the house doing to Mr. Sterling’s clients?

VALERIE
The property simply requires a specific maintenance fee. A closing cost.

Valerie looks down at her expensive sleeve. She begins scrubbing obsessively at the French cuff. There is no stain there. She scrubs harder.

VALERIE
I was just maximizing the asset. Every time it closed a deal... it produced these. The ultimate seller's market.

Toby’s trembling hand reaches out. He doesn't reach for her. He touches one of the diamonds. 

Slowly, methodically, Toby slides the gem along the grout line. He pulls another diamond over. He aligns them into a perfect ninety-degree angle.

TOBY
You... you curated the guest lists. You facilitated the showings.

VALERIE
I secured the exclusive listing!

Valerie crawls closer, weeping openly now. The icy professional mask shatters.

VALERIE
Do you know what happens when the market crashes, Toby? When the landlord changes the locks and your belongings are in trash bags on the curb? You freeze. You disappear.

Toby slides a third diamond into place. A perfect, right-angled grid. His breathing is shallow, rapid.

TOBY
The structural integrity... the blueprints didn't make sense because the floorplan shifts to trap them. You knew.

VALERIE
I thought if the foundation was expensive enough, it couldn't collapse on me! I thought this was bulletproof equity!

She points a shaking finger at the glittering stones. 

VALERIE
It was supposed to be a shield. But it just keeps depreciating the neighborhood.

A horrific, wet CRUNCH reverberates through the plumbing. The vanity mirror vibrates. 

Toby lines up the fourth diamond. He finally looks up from the perfect square.

TOBY
P-people are dying in the foyer, Valerie. The fixtures are eating them.

Valerie slumps against the marble vanity, utterly defeated. 

VALERIE
I know. We're completely out of inventory.

CUT TO:

scenes.narrative_action: Toby shrinks against the cold porcelain bathtub, overwhelmed. Under the supreme stress, a severe stutter overtakes his speech. His hands shake as he compulsively grabs three expensive bath bottles and aligns them into perfect, rigid right angles on the tub ledge. Finding a sudden shred of courage, he kicks the diamonds away. He forces Valerie to look at her bloodless, perfectly clean designer shoes, sharply rebuking her: her addiction to status is exactly what made her the house's powerless, obedient pet.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: The Catalyst delivers the thematic truth, breaking the Protagonist's lie. Micro-Conflict: Toby wants to hold Valerie accountable vs. Valerie's instinct to wallow in guilt. Value Shift: (+/-) From passive victim (-) to righteous moral authority (+).
scenes.draft_text:
INT. MANSION - MASTER BATHROOM - CONTINUOUS

Toby hits the cold porcelain of the freestanding bathtub. He slides down to the marble tile, knees pulled tight to his chest.

Scattered across the heated floor: a dozen flawless, blood-free diamonds.

Valerie stands over them. Her thumb aggressively, obsessively rubs the edge of her silk cuff. There is no stain there.

VALERIE
The appraisal on these... the clarity is unprecedented. We factor this into the escrow, we--

TOBY
(stuttering)
H-he's gone. The walls just... the f-framing shifted. A load-bearing beam cannot just unhinge like a jaw. It defies basic structural engineering.

VALERIE
(rubbing the cuff harder)
The acoustics in here are incredible. Vaulted ceilings really trap the sound. We'll market it as a private retreat.

Toby's hands shake violently. He reaches up to the tub ledge. 

He grabs a frosted glass shampoo bottle. Then a conditioner. Then an exfoliating scrub.

He slides them across the porcelain. Aligns them. Perfect, rigid right angles.

TOBY
I-it ate him, Valerie.

VALERIE
We are securing our financial future. That is the only metric that matters right now.

Toby stares at the perfectly aligned bottles. His jaw tightens. The stutter recedes behind a sudden, unfamiliar anger.

He stands up. His foot swings out.

He kicks the cluster of diamonds. 

They skitter violently across the tile, clinking down into the brass shower drain.

VALERIE
What are you doing?! Those are flawless!

Toby steps into her space. He points down at her feet.

TOBY
L-look at your shoes.

Valerie instinctively steps back, protecting her footwear.

VALERIE
They are custom Prada, Toby, back away.

TOBY
Look at them!

Valerie glances down. The designer leather is pristine. Not a single drop of blood.

TOBY
A man was just crushed into the floorboards. And you are completely clean.

Valerie swallows hard. Her thumb stops rubbing her cuff.

VALERIE
I am managing the asset.

TOBY
Y-you think you're the landlord. You think you hold the deed because you wear the silk.

Toby kicks another stray diamond toward the drain.

TOBY
You don't own this property. You're just the guard dog. It doesn't even need a fence to keep you here. It just drops a shiny rock on the floor, and you sit. You stay.

Valerie's icy veneer cracks. Her breath hitches.

TOBY
You're completely housebroken.

Toby turns his back on her. He walks toward the heavy oak door.

Valerie stands frozen. She stares down at her spotless shoes. 

The silence of the massive room swallows her.

CUT TO:

scenes.narrative_action: The harsh truth physically strikes Valerie. She stops crying, her breathing slowing as the paralyzing guilt transmuting into cold, selfless clarity. She stands up, deliberately crushing one of the diamonds under her heel, then kicks the rest down the shower drain. Refusing to die a passive victim, she grabs Toby's hand, pulls him to his feet, and demands to know the house's structural weakness.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: The protagonist's epiphany and active rejection of her fatal flaw. Micro-Conflict: Valerie's old, greedy self vs. her new, selfless clarity. Value Shift: (+/-) From broken and guilty (-) to resolved and lethal (+).
scenes.draft_text:
INT. MANSION - MASTER BATHROOM - CONTINUOUS

The Carrara marble floor vibrates. A deep, wet tearing sound echoes up through the brass plumbing.

Valerie sits slumped against the frosted glass of the shower. Her chest heaves in jagged, panicked rhythms. Tears cut through the plaster dust coating her cheeks.

Scattered across the tiles between her legs: a dozen flawless, blood-free diamonds. They gleam under the harsh vanity lights.

Toby kneels by the bidet. His hands shake violently. He slides three shattered pieces of mirror into a perfect, ninety-degree angle.

TOBY
(stuttering)
Th-the police. We have to... there's a county protocol for-for sudden structural collapse, we just need to file a--

Valerie's breath catches. She stares at the diamonds. The ultimate shield. The end of the evictions.

She looks down at her ruined silk cuff. Her thumb rubs frantically at a phantom stain on the fabric. Rubbing bone-deep.

Another wet crunch from the pipes below. The house is swallowing.

Valerie stops rubbing. Her trembling hands go entirely still.

The tears dry up. The paralyzing guilt drains from her face, replaced by a terrifying, cold clarity.

She stands. Slowly. Towering over the scattered gems.

TOBY
V-Valerie? We can't afford to--

Her designer heel comes down hard on the largest diamond.

CRUNCH.

The stone shatters into worthless, glittering dust against the tile.

Toby flinches, knocking his perfect mirror angles out of place.

Valerie steps forward, kicking the remaining diamonds. They slide across the wet porcelain and vanish down the open shower drain with a hollow rattle.

VALERIE
The market is over-saturated. We're terminating the listing.

She marches to Toby, grabs his trembling hand, and hauls him to his feet.

TOBY
(stumbling)
I-I can't. The doors are sealed. The biometric locks are--

VALERIE
Stop talking about the hardware.

She backs him against the vanity, eyes locked onto his.

VALERIE
You read the original permits. You spent three days obsessing over the zoning anomalies before we even staged the foyer.

TOBY
Y-yes, but the architect--

VALERIE
Every vanity project has a cut corner. A foundational flaw. Where is the structural compromise?

Toby stares at her. The sheer force of her focus acts like gravity. His stutter evaporates as his brain locks into the blueprints.

TOBY
The subterranean HVAC intake. It bypasses the secondary firewall and feeds directly over the primary gas main.

Valerie releases his hand. Her posture straightens. Lethal.

VALERIE
Show me the basement.

SMASH CUT TO:

scenes.narrative_action: Toby closes his eyes, his breathing syncing with the house's rhythmic thrum. He traces his fingers in the air, physically mapping out the uncorrupted structural engineering blueprints from his savant memory. He draws a complex line down to the floor, pinpointing the anomaly: a highly pressurized, vulnerable main gas line buried deep beneath the basement's shifting geometry.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: Locating the physical target for the Break into Three. Micro-Conflict: Toby's memory vs. the house's complex, shifting anatomy. Value Shift: (+/-) From clueless and trapped (-) to armed with architectural knowledge (+).
scenes.draft_text:
INT. MANSION - MASTER BATHROOM - CONTINUOUS

The marble floor hums. A low, rhythmic thrum, like a resting heartbeat.

TOBY presses his back against the frosted glass shower door. His chest heaves. 

He scrambles to the dual vanity. His trembling hands grab a row of heavy, slate apothecary jars. 

He shoves them into a line. Perfect ninety-degree angles. 

TOBY
(stuttering)
S-standard load-bearing... c-cantilevered...

A deep, bass-heavy vibration rattles the walls. The jars slide out of alignment. 

Toby stares at the crooked slate. His breathing hitches. 

He closes his eyes. 

He inhales. Exhales. He forces his lungs to match the exact, agonizing rhythm of the house's pulse. 

Toby raises his right hand. His index finger points at the empty space above the sink. 

He draws a sharp, invisible horizontal line in the air. 

TOBY
Primary HVAC artery. Two hundred millimeters. 

His finger drops, angling sharply to the left. He drafts a complex schematic across the empty room. 

TOBY
Secondary support beams. R-reinforced steel. 

He pivots. His eyes remain tightly shut. 

His hands move faster, conducting an invisible symphony of structural geometry. 

The bathroom walls groan. The ceiling lowers a fraction of an inch. The house is changing shape. 

Toby ignores the shifting space. His finger traces a thick, jagged path down the vanity mirror. 

He tracks the invisible line over the marble tiles, stopping directly between his own feet. 

TOBY
By-pass the shifting foundation. Underneath the sub-floor. 

He drops to his knees. He presses his ear flat against the cold marble. 

A violent, pressurized hiss echoes from deep beneath the floorboards. 

Toby opens his eyes. The panic is completely gone. 

TOBY
(whispering)
High-pressure gas main. Unregulated. 

He traces the grout line with his thumb. 

TOBY
I found your throat.

SMASH CUT TO:

scenes.narrative_action: Valerie paces the bathroom, realizing they need to expose the house's vital organs to reach the gas line. She taps into her ruthless corporate mindset, explaining that the house needs a massive distraction. She picks up one of Toby's perfectly aligned bottles and smashes it into the sink, declaring Richard as the ultimate bait. She outlines the tactical scheme to lure her predatory boss directly into the basement's digestive core.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: Synthesizing the demolition plan using Valerie's corporate ruthlessness for good. Micro-Conflict: Ethical hesitation vs. tactical necessity to use a human as bait. Value Shift: (+/-) From lacking a strategy (-) to possessing a lethal, unified plan (+).

scenes.scene_heading: INT. MANSION - UPSTAIRS HALLWAY - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.narrative_action: Toby stares at the shattered glass in the sink, his moral compass shifting as he gives a firm nod, aligning with Valerie's tactical cruelty. Valerie grips the deadbolt. They share a final look of unified resolve. Valerie throws the lock, pushing the heavy reinforced door open, and the two step out into the hostile, dimly lit hallway, ready to hunt down their boss.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: Crossing the threshold into Act III's execution phase. Micro-Conflict: The safety of the bathroom vs. the active danger of stepping back into the house. Value Shift: (+/-) From hiding in fear (-) to actively hunting their prey (+).

scenes.narrative_action: Valerie and Toby burst from the bathroom doorway into the dimly lit hallway. The floorboards immediately snap upward at their heels like wooden jaws. Valerie grabs Toby's arm, shoving him forward as they physically dodge the shifting, hostile architecture, sprinting desperately toward the grand staircase.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: To establish the lethal, ticking-clock environment of the house's digestion phase and transition the characters to the final battleground.

scenes.scene_heading: INT. MANSION - RUINED LIVING ROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.narrative_action: Reaching the ground floor, Valerie signals Toby to hide in the shadows. She scans the chaotic, crumbling living room and spots Richard cowering behind a shattered marble pillar, clutching a piece of sharp debris. Valerie raises her hands in mock surrender, stepping out into the open to draw his attention.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: To locate the final sacrifice and initiate the trap.

scenes.scene_heading: INT. MANSION - RUINED LIVING ROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.narrative_action: Valerie feigns breathless panic, screaming that the master safe and offshore accounts are hidden in the basement. Richard's sophisticated facade shatters completely. He drops his weapon, physically invades her personal space, and whispers a vicious threat through clenched teeth, demanding the exact location before violently shoving past her toward the basement door.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: To exploit the antagonist's fatal flaw (greed), successfully baiting him into the monster's maw.

scenes.narrative_action: Richard charges blindly down the narrow, descending staircase, his eyes wide with desperate greed. Valerie drops her panicked act instantly. Her expression goes ice cold as she silently slides down the staircase right behind him, her posture predatory as she ensures her prey doesn't turn back.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: To transition the characters into the belly of the beast, marking the point of no return.

scenes.narrative_action: Richard breaches the basement floor. Instantly, the concrete walls animate, violently slamming together to trap him. The floor beneath him splits wide open, revealing a grinding, horrific nexus of architectural gears and pulsing, fleshy mortar. Richard screams in agony as the shifting masonry begins grinding his legs into dust.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: To deliver the monster's final meal and brutally eliminate the primary human antagonist.

scenes.narrative_action: Valerie enters the shifting chamber, fighting a crushing gravitational pull dragging her toward the fleshy maw. She pulls the heavy bag of blood-free diamonds from her pocket. The extreme stress hits her; she obsessively scrubs at an invisible stain on her ruined cuff for two frantic seconds, then violently hurls her entire fortune directly into the grinding stomach.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: The protagonist's ultimate moral choice—sacrificing her hoarded wealth to destroy the corrupt system she once worshipped.

scenes.narrative_action: The indestructible diamonds hit the fleshy mortar and violently jam the supernatural gears. The basement walls shudder, screeching in agonizing, metallic pain as the house chokes on the gems. Valerie pulls a heavy brass lighter from her pocket, sparks the flame, and locks eyes with the hissing, ruptured gas lines Toby exposed earlier.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: To weaponize the house's own consumption against it and trigger the explosive finale.

scenes.narrative_action: Valerie tosses the burning lighter directly into the hissing gas cloud. She pivots and sprints toward a narrow, high basement window. She leaps, diving headfirst through the shattering glass just as the trapped gas detonates behind her in a massive, blinding fireball.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: The physical climax of the sequence, executing the protagonist's desperate escape from the blast radius.

scenes.narrative_action: Valerie crashes hard onto the manicured lawn. The explosive shockwave rolls over her battered body, blowing dirt and grass into the air. She forces herself up from the scorched earth, turning to watch the multi-million dollar modernist property become entirely engulfed in a furious, roaring inferno.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: To visually confirm the absolute destruction of the monster and the end of the immediate physical threat.

scenes.narrative_action: Standing safely on the curb, a soot-covered Valerie pats down her ruined designer suit. She checks her pockets—completely empty. The offshore fortune is gone. Instead of panicking, her shoulders drop. The icy professional mask she wore for decades permanently melts away into a serene, exhausted smile.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: To establish the protagonist's new emotional baseline, proving she has overcome her central lie regarding wealth and safety.

scenes.narrative_action: The deafening roar of the fire completely silences the house's dying screams. Valerie notices a bright red ladybug crawling on her torn sleeve. She gently scoops it up, watching it take flight into the cool night air. Stripped of her wealth and status, she turns her back on the smoldering ruins and walks away into the dark.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: The Final Image. To provide thematic closure, emphasizing her rejection of corporate greed and her return to humanity.
